yesMatch.biologicalState: Your smoke detector is accurate. Your energy matches the demand.
yesMatch.infoMatch: Your state matches the demand, simply use the energy.

noMatch.biologicalState: We only get stuck if our state doesn't match our demands.
noMatch.infoMatch: The mismatch is acting as an active barrier. Shift your physiology using one of the tools to change your state.

sensationData.title: Hibernating
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel like hibernating. Your tank is empty and your body has hit the emergency brake. You are safe to pause and thaw at your own pace.
sensationData.blueprint: Metabolic conservation mode. Your heart rate and breathing drop to preserve core vitality.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A profound, immovable heaviness; a drop in body temperature; the feeling of an empty chest cavity.
sensationData.metaphor: A machine cutting power to all non-essential systems to save the last drop of battery.
sensationData.theImpulse: To isolate, lie down, and retreat into a dark, quiet sanctuary.
sensationData.superpower: Biological Shielding. Your system is masterfully powering down to protect you from overwhelming stress.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel heavy and trapped in an internal void. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Sound Waves. Play activating music and let the vibrations fill the room.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using external vibrations to jumpstart a system that feels 'unplugged'.

mind.text: External Focus. Name 3 objects in the room and whisper their shapes.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Gently re-directing the 'Thinking Brain' toward objective reality to break the loop of despair.

social.text: Digital Ping. Text a safe person a single emoji to acknowledge the outside world.
social.physioTip: Best for: Breaking isolation with a zero-pressure signal, reminding the system that a social harbor exists.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You are spaced out and disconnected from the room. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Sensory Shock. Splash cold water on your face or feel the floor supporting your weight.
body.physioTip: Best for: A thermal or tactile 'reset' to bring the nervous system back into the present.

mind.text: Boundary Tracing. Mentally state: 'My feet end at the floor. My back ends at the chair.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Defining the edges of the 'self' to prevent the feeling of evaporating or fading away.

social.text: Internal Anchor. Place a hand over your heart and recall the feeling of a safe hug.
social.physioTip: Best for: Accessing somatic memory for co-regulation when physical company is unavailable.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Immobilized
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel immobilized. Your system prioritizes survival-stillness over action. You're not stuck forever; this is a pause button for safety.
sensationData.blueprint: Dorsal vagal dominance. Your parasympathetic system has taken full control, dropping your heart rate into a state of metabolic rest.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A profound, leaden heaviness. Movement feels like a mechanical impossibility.
sensationData.metaphor: A vehicle stalled on the tracks; the engine is cold, and the key won't turn, waiting for a jumpstart.
sensationData.theImpulse: To surrender, sink into the floor, and become entirely invisible.
sensationData.superpower: The Emergency Brake. Your body is numbing you to save you from unbearable distress, buying time until safety returns.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel heavy, numb, and trapped inside an internal void. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Auditory Spark. Put on an activating piece of music and let the sound fill the room.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using rhythmic vibrations to penetrate leaden heaviness and signal the brain that the environment is safe and moving.

mind.text: Shape Naming. Name 3 objects in the room and whisper what shape they are.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Gently re-engaging exteroception (external focus) to interrupt the internal void.

social.text: Text a safe person a single emoji just to ping the outside world.
social.physioTip: Best for: A low-stakes 'ping' of connection that requires zero verbal energy but confirms you aren't invisible.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You are spaced out and disconnected from the room. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Temperature Shock. Splash cold water on your face, or feel the solid surface supporting your weight.
body.physioTip: Best for: Forcing a 'bottom-up' sensory reset through the thermal or tactile receptors in the skin.

mind.text: Physical Boundaries. Mentally state: 'My feet end at the floor. My back ends at the chair.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Defining the physical container of the body when the sense of 'self' feels faded or thin.

social.text: Internal Anchor. Place a hand over your heart and imagine a safe hug, or focus on a pet nearby.
social.physioTip: Best for: Accessing non-verbal safety through somatic memory or the presence of a co-regulating person/pet.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Hollow
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel hollow. Depletion signals retreat. You are not broken; this void is a protocol protecting you from system failure.
sensationData.blueprint: Energy-conserving stasis. Your nervous system has ceased non-essential functions to conserve your last drops of fuel.
sensationData.physicalSensation: The physical feeling of an empty chest cavity; a lack of internal substance or life force.
sensationData.metaphor: A dried-up riverbed or a winter landscape where everything has gone dormant to survive the cold.
sensationData.theImpulse: To fade away, become invisible, and detach from an environment that feels barren.
sensationData.superpower: Total Conservation. Your system masterfully strips away all excess to preserve core vitality for eventual renewal.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel hollowed-out, an internal desert of depletion. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Friction Heat. Rub your palms against each other and feel the heat generate between them.
body.physioTip: Best for: Generating internal warmth and proprioceptive input to remind the system it is still physically present.

mind.text: Color Anchor. Name 3 things in the room that have the same color to pull your brain out of the void.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'internal desert' focus by forcing the eyes to scan and categorize the external environment.

social.text: Visual Resource. Look at a photo of a trusted person or pet. Acknowledge their existence quietly.
social.physioTip: Best for: Low-demand co-regulation using visual memories to refill the social 'tank' without requiring output.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You perceive a drained, barren world. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Breath Wave. Place one hand on your belly; feel the subtle rise and fall of breath for 5 cycles.
body.physioTip: Best for: Establishing a rhythmic internal anchor through gentle diaphragm movement.

mind.text: Object Description. Find one object and describe it in your head with three specific words.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Anchoring the thinking brain to a single, concrete reality to prevent the feeling of 'fading away'.

social.text: Self-Hug. Place your hands on opposite shoulders and squeeze firmly to remind your brain where your edges are.
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing 'proprioceptive closure'—using deep pressure to define the body's physical boundaries.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Empty
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel empty. You have given everything, and your system has now closed the doors to recharge.
sensationData.blueprint: Post-stress collapse. Metabolic resources are at 'Reserve' levels; the brain is muting signals to prevent further drain.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A hollow feeling in the solar plexus; a sensation of being a 'shell' with no internal substance.
sensationData.metaphor: An empty warehouse after the move—echoey, quiet, and bare.
sensationData.theImpulse: To lie flat, avoid conversation, and stare at a blank surface.
sensationData.superpower: The Blank Slate. This emptiness is a prerequisite for a clean, new beginning once you have rested.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The void feels heavy and bottomless. Goal: Gentle Anchoring.

body.text: Skin Contact. Place a hand on your chest and feel the subtle warmth of your skin.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using the body's own warmth to signal safety and presence to the heart-center.

mind.text: Gentle Observance. Simply notice the sensation of air entering your nostrils. No counting, just noticing.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Anchoring awareness to the body's most basic survival function without adding cognitive load.

social.text: Ambient Companion. Look at a plant, a shadow, or a neutral object and acknowledge its presence as a silent companion in your space.
social.physioTip: Best for: Breaking isolation through non-demanding, non-human connection.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You feel disconnected from your environment. Goal: Pull in.

body.text: Tactile Trace. Run your fingers along the texture of your clothing or the chair you are in.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using exteroception to define the physical boundary between 'self' and 'world'.

mind.text: Vocal Identity. State your name and your current location out loud in a whisper.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Re-orienting the 'Thinking Brain' to time and place after a period of dissociation.

social.text: Auditory Memory. Recall the sound of a safe person’s voice without needing to call them.
social.physioTip: Best for: Accessing co-regulation resources internally when external social energy is at zero.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Deflated
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel deflated. Your expectations met a roadblock, and your body dropped its tension to save battery.
sensationData.blueprint: Loss of internal pressure. You feel like a sail with no wind—heavy, limp, and dragging.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A lack of muscular 'tone'; shoulders and chest feel collapsed or hollowed out.
sensationData.metaphor: A balloon that has lost its helium; it’s still there, but it’s hovering an inch off the floor, unable to rise.
sensationData.theImpulse: To sigh deeply, drop your shoulders, and abandon the current task.
sensationData.superpower: Efficiency. Deflation masterfully conserves your final sparks by folding inward like a protective husk.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel heavy and drained. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Visual Lift. Use your eyes to trace the outline of a tall window or doorway to pull your posture up.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using the vestibulo-ocular reflex to gently encourage a more upright, alert posture.

mind.text: Relative Scale. Look around and out loud name 3 things that are physically larger than you.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting focus from internal smallness to the stable, large-scale reality of your environment.

social.text: Ambient Prosody. Turn on a podcast of a comforting voice; let the rhythm play in the background.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the safety frequency of a human voice to provide a social 'buffer' without interaction.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You feel flat and are staring blankly into space. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Thermal Contrast. If you have a drink nearby, take a slow sip. If not, splash cold water on your wrists or use a cool cloth.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using a strong internal thermal signal to wake up the vagus nerve and anchor into the body.

mind.text: Internal Rhythm. Close your eyes and see if you can count 5 of your own heartbeats.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Tuning into the body's most basic internal clock to regain a sense of self.

social.text: Battery Status. Text a safe person exactly what your internal battery percentage is right now.
social.physioTip: Best for: Practicing objective self-reporting to a safe harbor without needing to 'explain' why.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Frozen
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel frozen. Your system has detected a threat that feels inescapable, so it has crystallized your state to protect you from impact. You aren't 'stuck'; you are currently in a protective stasis.
sensationData.blueprint: High-Tone Dorsal Shutdown. The 'freeze' response has created a muscular and energetic 'shell'. Your metabolism has slowed to a crawl to preserve your vital resources.
sensationData.physicalSensation: Icy stillness; cold extremities; shallow, 'locked' breathing; and a heavy, immovable weight behind the eyes.
sensationData.metaphor: An arctic landscape mid-winter. Everything is preserved in ice, motionless and silent, holding its breath until the spring.
sensationData.theImpulse: To stay perfectly still, to stop time, and to avoid any movement that might 'break' the protective shell.
sensationData.superpower: Absolute Preservation. Your system is protecting your most precious internal resources. You are a biological time-capsule, safe from a harsh outside world.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are trapped in the internal ice. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Edge Scan. Touch the edge of a hard object (table, phone, chair). Trace the cold, clear boundary of the object with your index finger.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using 'boundary data' to pull awareness out of the internal void and back into the physical room.

mind.text: Color Hunting. Find three items in the room that are NOT white or grey. Say their colors out loud.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'internal monochrome' of a frozen state by forcing the visual cortex to process high-contrast external data.

social.text: Safe Proximity. If a safe person or pet is nearby, simply look at their feet or their back. Notice their solid presence in the room with you.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'passive co-regulation' to tether your nervous system to a safe external living signal.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels cold and unreachable. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Core Thaw. Gently rub your palms together to create friction-warmth, then place them directly over your heart or solar plexus.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing a localized 'heat signal' to the chest to encourage the return of blood flow to the core.

mind.text: The Internal Flame. Visualize a single, steady candle flame at the very center of your chest. Feel its tiny radius of warmth.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Creating a safe, micro-focused internal anchor to help re-inhabit the body without feeling overwhelmed.

social.text: The Internal Lighthouse. Close your eyes and visualize the face of someone who makes you feel safe. Imagine them looking at you with a gentle smile.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using 'introjects' (internalized safety figures) to create a warm internal social climate.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Shutdown
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel shut down. Numbness isn't a lack of feeling; it’s a high-level protective gear your brain wears to prevent a total system crash. You are not broken; your body is successfully executing an ancient survival protocol.
sensationData.blueprint: The 'Vault' response. Your system has determined the environment is too costly to engage with. It has pulled the emergency brake to protect your vital organs.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A heavy, leaden weight throughout the body. A sense of being 'switched off' or disconnected from the motor controls.
sensationData.metaphor: A deep-sea creature retreating into a dark trench where the pressure is immense, but the world above cannot reach it.
sensationData.theImpulse: To curl into a ball, pull the covers over your head, and completely disappear from view.
sensationData.superpower: Absolute Containment. You are currently a fortress. Your system is prioritizing core survival by cutting off all external noise.
sensationData.generalGoal: The 'Thaw'. A tiny pulse of presence.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel heavy and trapped in the 'Vault'. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The 180-Degree Horizon. Without moving your head, slowly let your eyes drift to the furthest left and furthest right points of the room.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using the 'peripheral scan' to tell the brainstem the room is safe and open, gently nudging the system out of the 'hidden' shutdown state.

mind.text: The Vocal Pulse. Count backward from 10 out loud. Try to feel the vibration of your own voice in the air around you.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'internal silence' of shutdown by using the vocal cords to occupy physical space and signal presence.

social.text: The Audio Anchor. Turn on a podcast or music with a warm, melodic human voice (prosody). Focus on the 'rhythm' of the talk, not the content.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'passive social engagement'—receiving safety signals through vocal tones without the burden of interacting.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You feel spaced out and detached. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Palate Press. Press your tongue firmly against the roof of your mouth. Notice the exact texture and the pressure on your hard palate.
body.physioTip: Best for: Activating the trigeminal nerve and 'midline' receptors to pull the brain back into the physical structure of the head.

mind.text: Thermal Eye-Press. Rub your palms together to create heat, then cup them over your closed eyes. Feel the darkness and the warmth seep in.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using 'compression and heat' to provide a high-contrast internal signal that anchors the user back into their own skull.

social.text: Internal Witness. Acknowledge your own heartbeat or the weight of your own hands. Say silently: 'I am back inside this body.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Re-establishing 'self-as-anchor,' confirming that the physical body is a safe and occupied territory.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Numb
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel numb. Numbness is a biological anesthetic; your brain has turned down the volume on all input to save you from feeling too much at once.
sensationData.blueprint: Sensory Dampening. The Dorsal Vagus is muting your feedback loops. This mechanism prevents emotional or physical overwhelm from reaching your processor.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A 'cotton-wool' feeling in the head; a lack of tactile sensation in the skin; a feeling of being 'buffered' from the air.
sensationData.metaphor: Walking through the world wearing a thick diving suit. You can see the world, but you can't feel the breeze.
sensationData.theImpulse: To disconnect, to drift, and to remain in the 'gray' where nothing can touch you.
sensationData.superpower: Impact Protection. You can exist in high-stress environments without absorbing the blow. You are in 'Armor' mode.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel lost in a gray void. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Temperature Shock. Find something cold (a glass of water, a stone, or a metal spoon). Hold it firmly and focus on the exact edge where the cold meets your skin.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using thermal contrast to pierce through the 'sensory buffer' and force the thalamus to process external data.

mind.text: The Eye Trace. Pick a complex object in the room. Trace its entire outline with your eyes as slowly as possible, as if drawing it in the air.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from 'vague scanning' to 'active tracking,' which re-engages the visual cortex with the external environment.

social.text: The Rhythmic Ping. Tap a clear rhythm on a hard surface (table/wall). Listen for the distinct 'click' it makes in the room.
social.physioTip: Best for: Synchronizing an internal action with an external sound to 'mark' your presence in the shared space.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like a silent movie. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Forearm Grip. Grip your own forearms firmly. Squeeze until you can feel the density of the muscle and the resistance of the bone underneath.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing high-intensity deep pressure (DTP) to provide the 'buffer' brain with a clear map of where the body begins.

mind.text: Heel-Loading. Press your heels firmly into the ground. Identify the exact spot on your feet where the most pressure is located.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing gravity and skeletal loading to 'pull' the awareness back down into the vertical axis of the body.

social.text: Tactile Memory Trace. Close your eyes. Recall exactly how a specific texture feels (e.g., a pet's fur or a favorite sweater). Try to feel it 'internally'.
social.physioTip: Best for: Stimulating the somatosensory cortex through 'emotive simulation' to re-sensitize the internal map of the self.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Drained
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel drained. You've been running on 'E' for too long, and your system has forced a shutdown to prevent permanent engine damage.
sensationData.blueprint: Resource Bankruptcy. Your sympathetic fuel is gone. Your body is now enforcing a mandatory metabolic recharge to prevent total collapse.
sensationData.physicalSensation: Burning or aching in the muscles; dry eyes; a 'fuzzy' perimeter to your thoughts and vision.
sensationData.metaphor: A smartphone battery at 1%. The screen is dimmed, apps are closed, and only emergency calls are possible.
sensationData.theImpulse: To stop all decision-making, close your eyes mid-sentence, and sleep indefinitely.
sensationData.superpower: Radical Honesty. Drained is the body’s most honest boundary. It is the wisdom that knows when 'more' is no longer possible.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the ache of depletion. Goal: Internal Softening.

body.text: Jaw Drop. Scan your jaw. If it's tight, give it permission to 'drop' just 2 millimeters.
body.physioTip: Best for: Releasing the 'holding pattern' of chronic stress to initiate the parasympathetic recovery response.

mind.text: Permission Slip. Give yourself permission to think about 'nothing' for 60 seconds.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Halting the 'Software' drain of decision-making and cognitive loops.

social.text: Internal Shielding. Imagine a 'Do Not Disturb' sign hanging on your internal door.
social.physioTip: Best for: Establishing a symbolic boundary to protect remaining social energy.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The room feels too bright and fast. Goal: Dimming the Input.

body.text: Eye Palming. Cup your palms over your eyes to create total darkness for 20 seconds.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing an immediate sensory 'blackout' to soothe an overstimulated optic nerve.

mind.text: Low-Resolution Sight. Soften your gaze until everything in the room looks slightly blurry and out of focus.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Switching from 'alert' focal vision to 'safe' peripheral vision to lower sympathetic arousal.

social.text: Offline Status. Text a safe person: 'Offline for a bit. Rest mode.' This protects your energy.
social.physioTip: Best for: Practicing agency by announcing a boundary, reducing the anxiety of 'missing' social demands.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Lethargic
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel lethargic. Your system has shifted into a low-power motor mode to prevent you from over-extending.
sensationData.blueprint: Metabolic Braking. The dorsal vagal system is slowing down your motor output to conserve what little energy is left.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A heavy, glue-like feeling in the muscles. Movement feels like walking through deep mud.
sensationData.metaphor: A clock with a battery so low that the hands are barely ticking. The mechanism is fine, but the current is weak.
sensationData.theImpulse: To move as little as possible, speak in a monotone, and remain slumped or reclined.
sensationData.superpower: Energy Safeguard. You are preventing a total system crash by moving only at a pace your battery can support.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel heavy and sluggish. Goal: External Pulse.

body.text: Toe Taps. Slowly tap your toes against the floor, alternating left and right.
body.physioTip: Best for: Stimulating the most distant motor nerves to invite a tiny pulse of circulation back to the core.

mind.text: The Metal Scan. Count 5 objects in the room made of metal.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the brain to categorize specific physical properties, pulling focus out of the heavy internal fog.

social.text: Minimal Signal. Send a single emoji to a safe person just to stay tethered.
social.physioTip: Best for: Maintaining social presence with the absolute minimum amount of metabolic output.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too fast and demanding. Goal: Internal Anchor.

body.text: The Swallowing Sip. Take a very small sip of water and notice the sensation of swallowing.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using a high-sensitivity internal movement to reconnect with the midline of the body.

mind.text: Audio Scan. Name three things you can hear right now, no matter how quiet.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Transitioning from 'shutdown' to 'passive listening,' a key step in re-entering the social engagement system.

social.text: Safe Imagery. Imagine sitting by a slow-moving river, letting the world float past.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using symbolic imagery to provide a sense of 'flow' that matches your current low-energy capacity.

mind.text: Audio Scan. Name three things you can hear right now, no matter how quiet.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Transitioning from 'shutdown' to 'passive listening,' a key step in re-entering the social engagement system.

social.text: Safe Imagery. Imagine sitting by a slow-moving river, letting the world float past.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using symbolic imagery to provide a sense of 'flow' that matches your current low-energy capacity.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Disconnected
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel disconnected. When the environment feels unsafe or hostile, your brain protects you by 'unplugging' the data feed. You aren't losing your mind; you are using a biological shield.
sensationData.blueprint: Dissociative buffering. Your nervous system is muting the connection between your 'self' and your surroundings to reduce the emotional impact of a perceived threat.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A feeling of being 'behind glass' or looking at the world from far away. Your own voice may sound distant, and your limbs might feel like they don't quite belong to you.
sensationData.metaphor: A radio station with a weak signal—there is a lot of static, and the 'real' broadcast feels like it's happening in another room.
sensationData.theImpulse: To stare blankly, to drift into daydreams, and to avoid eye contact or any interaction that requires 'presence'.
sensationData.superpower: The Great Buffer. You have the ability to survive overwhelming environments by distancing your core self from the immediate chaos.
sensationData.generalGoal: Tactile grounding. Finding one physical tether.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel lost in an internal fog or a 'void'. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Surface Press. Press your palms firmly against a cold wall or a hard table. Notice the resistance of the object and the temperature of the surface.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using external resistance to provide a clear 'edge' where the body ends and the world begins, pulling awareness out of the internal void.

mind.text: The Identity Anchor. State your full name, the current day of the week, and your exact location out loud. Listen to the sound of your voice in the room.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using the 'Acoustic Reflex' and vocalization to anchor the self to a specific time and physical coordinate in the environment.

social.text: The Texture Hunt. Pick one object in the room that you didn't notice before. Name its color and texture (e.g., 'That blue cushion is rough').
social.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the brain to categorize and label external data, which interrupts the dissociative drift.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like a movie you aren't part of. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Humerus Squeeze. Cross your arms and grip your own upper arms (humerus) firmly. Squeeze for 5 seconds, feel the bone, and release.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing 'Deep Pressure Input' to the large muscles of the arms to remind the brain it is safely inside a physical container.

mind.text: The Hand Trace. Look at your own palm. Use one finger to slowly trace the lines of your hand. Say silently: 'This is my hand, I am here.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Establishing 'Self-Affiliation'—visually and physically confirming the reality of your own body to counter de-personalization.

social.text: Internal Safety Memory. Recall exactly how a specific, safe texture feels (e.g., a pet's fur or a heavy blanket). Try to feel it 'internally' on your skin.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using 'affective touch' memories to stimulate the insula and re-tether the self to internal biological reality.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Collapsed
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel collapsed. The demands became too heavy, and your body initiated a protective drop in energy to keep you safe. You are not weak; your biology is executing a damage-control protocol.
sensationData.blueprint: Postural surrender. Your system has rapidly lost its sympathetic tone, causing your muscles to go slack to prevent a complete system fracture.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A sinking feeling in the chest; slumped shoulders; a heavy chin. It feels like your bones have turned to lead and gravity has doubled.
sensationData.metaphor: A tent that has lost its center pole in a storm; the fabric is still there, but the structure has given way to the weight of the rain.
sensationData.theImpulse: To drop to the floor, curl into a ball, weep quietly, or surrender entirely to gravity.
sensationData.superpower: Surrender. Your system wisely pulled the plug to prevent a break, allowing the storm to pass over you rather than through you.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel physically folded inward and heavy. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Window Trace. Slowly uncurl your spine just one inch and lift your chin. Use your eyes to trace the vertical outline of a doorway or window from top to bottom.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing the 'head-eye' reflex to gently re-engage the deep spinal extensors without requiring high-energy output.

mind.text: The External Constant. Identify one large, unmoving object in the room (e.g., a heavy cupboard, a wall). Say silently: 'That is solid, that is there.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Providing a stable external 'anchor' when the internal sense of structure feels like it has given way.

social.text: The Reliable Witness. Mentally name one person or animal who has remained present with you during difficult times. Visualize their face in the room with you.
social.physioTip: Best for: Recalling 'unconditional presence' to mitigate the shame-spiral often triggered by postural collapse.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too bright, loud, or close. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Arm Squeeze. Use your right hand to firmly squeeze down your left arm, from shoulder to wrist. Feel the bone underneath the muscle. Repeat on the other side.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using 'Deep Pressure Input' to create a somatic container, helping the brain perceive the physical limits of the self.

mind.text: The Breath Column. Inhale slowly and notice the internal pressure of the air against your ribs and back. Feel it acting as a temporary 'internal pole'.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the diaphragm to create intra-abdominal pressure, which provides a visceral sense of stability from the inside out.

social.text: Maintenance Permission. Imagine a 'Closed for Maintenance' sign on your door. Tell yourself: 'It is safe to be offline right now. I am recharging.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Granting symbolic permission for metabolic recharge, reducing the 'drain' caused by social-monitoring neurons.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Withdrawn
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel withdrawn. Your system is curating its energy by 'unplugging' from social demands. This is a strategic conservation of your remaining resources.
sensationData.blueprint: Social engagement shutdown. Your nervous system has assessed that the 'cost' of interacting is too high, so it has pulled you into a protective inner sanctum.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A feeling of being 'tucked in' or wearing an invisible cloak. You may notice your voice is quieter and your gaze shifts downward.
sensationData.metaphor: A turtle that has pulled into its shell—not because it's mean, but because the world feels a bit too loud for its skin today.
sensationData.theImpulse: To cancel plans, move to a smaller room, and avoid being 'seen' or 'needed'.
sensationData.superpower: Deep Introspection. In this shell, you have the quietest environment possible to hear your own internal truth.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel isolated in the dark of your own shell. Goal: Ambient Connection.

body.text: The Sound Tap. Tap your fingers against a cup or table and focus entirely on the sound the tapping makes.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using rhythmic sound and vibration to pierce the internal quiet without overstimulating the system.

mind.text: The Single Glimmer. Find one object that is your favorite color and notice how the light hits it.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Narrowing the visual focus to a single 'pleasure cue' to lower the cognitive load of scanning a full room.

social.text: The Window. Go sit by a window and watch cars or birds pass. Zero interaction required.
social.physioTip: Best for: Engaging in 'passive social witness'—acknowledging the world moves without the pressure to participate.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like a distant movie. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Rhythmic Rock. Hug your knees loosely and rock forward and backward slightly.
body.physioTip: Best for: Stimulating the vestibular system through low-frequency movement to induce a natural self-soothing response.

mind.text: Internal Temp Scan. Identify the warmest point in your body and the coldest point.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Tuning into specific internal thermal data to anchor awareness back inside the physical container.

social.text: The Tether. Imagine a thread of light connecting you to one safe person. You don't have to talk, just be tethered.
social.physioTip: Best for: Maintaining an internal 'social tether' when external communication feels too metabolically expensive.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Sinking
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are sinking. When progress feels stalled, your body lets go of its 'upward' tension to save fuel for the long haul.
sensationData.blueprint: Gravity surrender. Your sympathetic 'up' energy has dipped, allowing you to descend into a heavy, metabolic rest state.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A dragging feeling in the pelvis and legs, as if your chair is made of quicksand. Your chest feels heavy and your breath feels deep and slow.
sensationData.metaphor: An anchor slowly descending into deep water—away from the turbulent surface and into the stillness of the bottom.
sensationData.theImpulse: To stop trying, to let your body go limp, and to slide down until you can't go any lower.
sensationData.superpower: Finding the Floor. You cannot sink forever; eventually, you hit the bottom, and that becomes the solid ground you can push off from.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are descending into heavy internal depth. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Horizon Finding. Find one long horizontal line in the room (a shelf, a floorboard). Follow it slowly with your eyes from left to right and back again.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using a stable environmental horizon to provide the brain with 'level' data, preventing the internal sensation of sinking further.

mind.text: Buoyancy Imagery. Visualize one thing that naturally floats (a balloon, a cork, a cloud). See it bobbing effortlessly on the surface of the water.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Priming the brain with 'upward' concepts to gently shift the metabolic tone away from vertical descent.

social.text: The External Lift. Think of one specific person who 'elevates' the energy in a room just by walking in. Visualize their face and their posture.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'social mirroring' neurons to spark a tiny pulse of sympathetic 'up' energy from a safe external source.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You feel heavy or adrift from external pressure. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Heel-Strike Resistance. Press your heels firmly into the ground. Feel the floor 'pushing back' against your skeleton. Acknowledge the solid resistance.
body.physioTip: Best for: Activating the posterior chain and mechanoreceptors in the feet to establish 'the bottom' and a solid base for the self.

mind.text: Finding the Floor. Let your weight drop fully into the chair for 3 breaths. Mentally locate the exact points where your body meets the support.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the system to register that the descent has stopped because the floor/chair is successfully holding the load.

social.text: Shoulder Loading. If safe, place your own hands firmly on your shoulders and push down slightly. Feel the 'containment' and the weight.
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing deep pressure input to the 'top' of the system to signal that you are physically held and grounded from within.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Exhausted
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel exhausted. Your system is ringing the 'empty' bell to prevent you from doing permanent damage to your internal engine.
sensationData.blueprint: Metabolic bankruptcy. You have spent more than you have earned, and your system is enforcing a mandatory 'spending freeze'.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A burning or aching in the muscles; heavy, dry eyes; a 'fuzzy' or blurred perimeter to your thoughts.
sensationData.metaphor: A runner who has crossed the finish line and simply dropped to the grass. The race is over; the recovery has begun.
sensationData.theImpulse: To close your eyes mid-sentence, to stop all decision-making, and to sleep for an indefinite amount of time.
sensationData.superpower: The Biological Truth. Exhaustion is your body's most honest boundary. It is the wisdom that knows when 'more' is no longer possible.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the internal ache of depletion. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Soft Release. Scan your jaw (TMJ) and your lower belly. If you find tightness, give those specific spots permission to 'drop' for 10 seconds.
body.physioTip: Best for: Halting 'covert bracing' patterns that consume metabolic energy even when the system is supposedly resting.

mind.text: The Privacy Shield. Imagine a clear, semi-permeable 'Do Not Disturb' bubble around your chest and head. It keeps out all noise and expectations.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Reducing the 'social monitoring' cost in the prefrontal cortex, allowing the 'Self' to occupy space without being 'available'.

social.text: The Out-of-Office Ping. Text a safe person: 'Rest mode on. Offline for a while.' Acknowledge your boundary out loud to the room.
social.physioTip: Best for: Externalizing the 'Spending Freeze' to remove the background stress of social guilt or perceived demands.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too bright or fast for your tired eyes. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Eye Palming. Cup your palms over your closed eyes to create 100% darkness for 30 seconds. Feel the warmth of your hands on your face.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing a total blackout for the optic nerve to immediately down-regulate the sympathetic system and pull awareness back to the midline.

mind.text: Weighted Gravity. Focus on the weight of your clothes or a blanket on your shoulders. Mentally give 100% of your weight to the chair.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using 'passive grounding' to pull a spaced-out, tired brain back into the physical structure of the body.

social.text: Zero-Tasking Presence. Close your eyes. Notice only the internal rhythm of your heartbeat. Say silently: 'I am back inside, and I am resting.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Re-establishing an internal relationship of 'care' with the tired self, moving from 'doing' to 'inhabiting'.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Fading
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are fading. When the world feels hostile and your energy is low, your system begins to dim your presence to make you less of a target.
sensationData.blueprint: Hypo-arousal transition. Your nervous system is lowering the 'brightness' of your consciousness and motor drive because it perceives that being fully present is too risky or exhausting.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A sense of becoming 'translucent' or light-headed. Vision may feel slightly soft or out of focus, and your internal 'voice' feels quiet and far away.
sensationData.metaphor: A sunset behind a thick bank of clouds; the light is still there, but the colors are draining away into a dull gray.
sensationData.theImpulse: To stop talking mid-sentence, to drift away from the conversation, and to physically move toward the shadows or the edge of the room.
sensationData.superpower: Stealth Preservation. You are expertly minimizing your 'biological footprint' to wait for a safer time to shine.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel like you are disappearing into your own mind. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Ground Stomp. Stomp your feet on the floor twice, quite hard. Feel the vibration travel up to your knees.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using high-impact vibration to 'wake up' the lower limbs and anchor the self into the ground.

mind.text: Micro-Reading. Find one object with text and read the smallest word you can see.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the eyes into 'focal vision' to interrupt the soft-focus drift of fading.

social.text: The Name Call. Say your own name out loud in a firm, clear voice.
social.physioTip: Best for: Re-establishing personal identity and presence through the vibration of your own vocal cords.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like it's drifting away from you. Goal: Sensory Re-attachment.

body.text: The Temperature Reset. Wash your hands with cold water and focus entirely on the sensation of the water hitting your skin.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using thermal shock to bypass the 'fading' consciousness and force a sensory re-entry.

mind.text: Corner Count. Count the number of right angles (corners) you can see in your current view.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the geometric-analytical brain to provide structure when reality feels 'soft' or blurry.

social.text: Feature Focus. Look at a photo of a safe person and mentally list three specific things you like about their face.
social.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from vague isolation to high-detail relational connection using safe visual cues.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Slumped
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel slumped. Your system has stopped 'holding' you upright against a world that feels heavy and discouraging.
sensationData.blueprint: Postural deflation. The sympathetic 'tone' that keeps our spine erect has been turned down by the dorsal vagus, allowing gravity to take the lead.
sensationData.physicalSensation: Curved shoulders, a drooping head, and a heavy pelvis. It feels like your muscles have lost their 'spring' and are just hanging off your bones.
sensationData.metaphor: A sunflower at the end of a long, rain-heavy day—bowing its head because the weight of the water is more than it can carry.
sensationData.theImpulse: To slide down in your chair, to rest your head in your hands, and to sigh deeply while looking at the floor.
sensationData.superpower: Yielding to Gravity. You are resting the very muscles that usually have to work the hardest. You are letting the earth hold you for a moment.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel physically and emotionally folded in. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Horizon Lift. Slowly lift your gaze from the floor to the furthest point you can see in the room or out a window. Let your chin follow your eyes.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using the vestibulo-ocular reflex to gently lead the cervical spine into extension, signaling the brain to shift from 'internal hiding' to 'environmental scanning'.

mind.text: High-Point Scan. Identify three objects in the room that are physically 'high up' (a ceiling lamp, a high shelf, the top corner of a door).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'downward gaze' habit of morose states by forcing the oculomotor muscles to work in the vertical plane, which triggers postural alertness.

social.text: External Rhythmic Tap. Find a steady external sound (a clock, a distant bird, or a slow song). Tap your index finger to that rhythm, acknowledging the world's pace.
social.physioTip: Best for: Entraining the motor system to an external rhythm to pull the nervous system out of a 'stalled' internal loop.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like it is pressing down on you. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Palm Flip. While seated, simply turn your palms upward to face the ceiling. Notice how your humerus rotates and your chest widens internally.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing the kinetic chain of the arm to facilitate external rotation of the shoulders and open the thoracic cage without significant muscular effort.

mind.text: The Crown String. Imagine a thin, golden string attached to the very top of your head, gently lifting your skull by just one millimeter from the inside.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using ideokinesis (mental imagery of movement) to re-activate the deep postural stabilizers (multifidus) along the spine without triggering defensive tension.

social.text: The Internal Pillar. Visualize a safe, supportive presence (a friend or pet) standing right behind you, placing a gentle, warm hand between your shoulder blades.
social.physioTip: Best for: Accessing safe attachment memories to provide a symbolic 'internal pillar' of support for the upper back, reducing the feeling of being unprotected.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Shrinking
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are shrinking. Making yourself smaller is a brilliant biological way to reduce your exposure to stress.
sensationData.blueprint: Protective Coiling. Your nervous system is signaling your muscles to pull inward toward your core (the 'fetal' tendency) to protect your vital organs from perceived social or emotional pressure.
sensationData.physicalSensation: Tightness in the chest, pulling the knees together, and tucking the chin. You feel 'small' inside your clothes.
sensationData.metaphor: A piece of wool shrinking in hot water; becoming tighter, denser, and less expansive.
sensationData.theImpulse: To cross your arms, hide your hands, and avoid taking up space in the room.
sensationData.superpower: Core Protection. You are masterfully guarding your most vulnerable parts. You are becoming a 'harder target' while you regroup.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel tight and small internally. Goal: Width Awareness.

body.text: The Wing Span. Stretch your arms out wide to the sides, as if you were measuring a large box, then let them drop.
body.physioTip: Best for: Gently interrupting flexor dominance by activating the extensor muscles of the upper back and shoulders.

mind.text: Space Permission. Say silently: 'I am allowed to take up this much space.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Reducing the cognitive 'shame' response that often accompanies the physical urge to hide.

social.text: Power Recall. Recall a time you felt powerful or 'big' (e.g., after a workout or a success).
social.physioTip: Best for: Using somatic memory to re-ignite a sense of agency and physical presence.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The room feels too big or intimidating. Goal: Spatial Grounding.

body.text: The Outer Thigh Press. Place your hands on the outer edges of your thighs and push outward against them.
body.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the lateral line of the body to provide a sense of 'sturdy' external boundaries.

mind.text: Distance Estimation. Count how many steps it would take to walk to the nearest wall.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Re-calculating your relationship to the physical environment to lower the 'intimidation' of the space.

social.text: The Anchor Object. Find one object in the room that looks 'solid' and 'unshakeable'. Focus on it for 10 seconds.
social.physioTip: Best for: Outsourcing stability to an external object when the internal sense of 'solidness' is low.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Heavy
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel heavy or sad. Your body is lowering your pace so you can process loss or disappointment without crashing. You are not broken. Your system is taking a necessary pause to process and heal.
sensationData.blueprint: Metabolic conservation. Your system has slowed your heart rate and muscle tone to help you endure emotional weight, prioritizing internal 'processing' over external 'doing'.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A dragging weight in the limbs; a 'thick' feeling in the air; the sensation that your shoes are made of lead.
sensationData.metaphor: Walking through knee-deep water or wearing a lead vest. Every movement requires a conscious decision and extra fuel.
sensationData.theImpulse: To let the head hang, let the shoulders drop, move slowly, sigh often, and seek a soft place to land.
sensationData.superpower: Depth and grounding. This heaviness forces a pause that allows for deep metabolic and emotional processing. You are honoring what matters.
sensationData.generalGoal: Gentle lift. Lighten the load. Subtle unburdening.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel weighed down by a thick, internal cloud of sadness. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Counter-Press. Press palms flat on a table, push down firmly while lifting your chest slightly—feel the counterforce expand your posture.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using the ground reaction force to re-engage postural extensors and create 'buoyancy' in the ribcage.

mind.text: Texture Anchor. Silently name the exact texture of the fabric touching your skin right now (rough, soft, cool, fuzzy).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting focus from heavy internal emotions to immediate, neutral tactile data.

social.text: The Mental Scrap. Mentally name one specific thing you appreciate about a loved one or a pet.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using 'micro-gratitude' to spark a tiny pulse of oxytocin, softening the internal weight.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels gray, like a distant movie you aren't part of. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Breath Wave. Place a hand on your chest and one on your belly. Feel the rise and fall of your breath for 5 cycles.
body.physioTip: Best for: Re-establishing a rhythmic internal anchor to counteract the feeling of being 'adrift' or disconnected.

mind.text: Sound Shape. Focus on one sound in the room and try to describe its 'shape' in your mind (e.g., sharp, round, fuzzy).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the associative cortex to transform passive hearing into active, grounding exteroception.

social.text: The Self-Squeeze. Give yourself a gentle, firm squeeze on the opposite arms or shoulders.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using 'somatic containment' to define the body's edges when the world feels too vast or gray.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Flat
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel flat or bored. Your brain is successfully recognizing a lack of meaningful stimulation and is prompting you to seek a better use of your energy. You are not lazy.
sensationData.blueprint: Under-stimulation response. Your nervous system has assessed the environment as lacking reward or novelty, causing a mild drop in energy to 'idle' mode.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A lack of 'spark' or appetite for any activity. Eyes feel slightly glazed, and your posture feels neutral but uninspired.
sensationData.metaphor: A car idling in a driveway, burning fuel without going anywhere, or a radio tuned to a station playing nothing but quiet static.
sensationData.theImpulse: To scroll mindlessly, seek quick dopamine (junk food), or disengage completely from the present moment.
sensationData.superpower: Catalyst for Change. Boredom is a biological signal that you are ready for more. It is the friction that prompts creativity and movement.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel apathetic and unmotivated. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Seated Twist. While sitting, rotate your torso gently to look over one shoulder, then the other.
body.physioTip: Best for: Stimulating the spinal nerves and mechanoreceptors to 'wake up' the system without taxing low energy reserves.

mind.text: Novelty Search. Find one object you haven't looked at in a long time and notice three brand-new details about it.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Re-engaging the prefrontal cortex and dopamine pathways through the intentional search for novelty.

social.text: The Interest Ping. Send a quick message to a friend asking about a topic they are passionate about.
social.physioTip: Best for: Shifting focus toward external social narratives to break the loop of internal apathy.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You are mindlessly searching the external world for a dopamine hit. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Heat Press. Rub your palms together for 5 seconds to create heat, then press them firmly against your chest.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using thermal input to ground awareness back into the physical container, interrupting the 'mindless drift'.

mind.text: The Sensation Scan. Locate exactly where the feeling of 'flatness' lives in your body (e.g., throat, eyes, chest).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Transforming a vague 'mood' into a specific physical sensation, which makes it easier for the brain to process.

social.text: The Laughter Memory. Recall a time you laughed until it hurt. Try to feel that laughter in your belly for 10 seconds.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing somatic memory to trigger a minor release of endorphins and soften emotional flatness.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Retreating
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel like retreating. Your system is identifying a lack of safety and is proactively pulling back to prevent further exposure. You are not being 'difficult'; you are being protective.
sensationData.blueprint: Proactive withdrawal. A mild dorsal vagal shift where the body prepares to shut the gates before a full drain occurs.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A subtle pulling back in the chest, shoulders turning away from the center, and a desire to look at the exit.
sensationData.metaphor: The tide pulling away from the shore, leaving the sand exposed and quiet.
sensationData.theImpulse: To leave the room, stop the conversation, or physically turn your back to the environment.
sensationData.superpower: Boundaried Wisdom. You are noticing a lack of safety early, allowing you to choose your exit before your battery hits zero.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the urge to hide within yourself. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Neutral Gaze. Find one object in the room that is 'neutral' (e.g., a door handle, a plain chair) and look at it steadily for 15 seconds.
body.physioTip: Best for: Pulling awareness out of the internal 'disgust' loop by tethering the visual system to an emotionally empty object.

mind.text: The Furniture Count. Count the number of 'legs' on the furniture currently in your view. Focus on the hard, physical reality of the room.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the 'task-positive network' to anchor the brain in the external environment, reducing the urge to retreat internally.

social.text: The Buffer Step. If you are standing or sitting near others, physically shift back by one foot. Notice the feeling of reclaiming your own space.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using movement to provide the brainstem with a clear 'spatial safety' cue, proving you are not trapped.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels abrasive or repelling. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Fetal Lock. Cross your arms and tuck your hands firmly into your opposite armpits. Feel the heat and the solid containment of your own frame.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing deep pressure and thermal input to the chest and axilla to signal internal safety, pulling awareness back into the physical container.

mind.text: The Invisible Wall. Imagine a clear, solid glass wall or a protective bubble between you and the environment. See it as an impenetrable boundary.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Creating a symbolic cognitive 'filter' that allows you to remain physically present while mentally inhabiting a private, safe space.

social.text: The 30-Second Pause. In any interaction, give yourself permission to wait 30 seconds before responding. Feel the 'pause' as an internal room you live in.
social.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the metabolic drain of 'social masking' by establishing an internal sanctuary of time and silence.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Sagging
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel sagging. The internal pressure that holds you upright has dipped slightly, inviting you to lower your metabolic output.
sensationData.blueprint: Mild postural release. Your sympathetic tone is fluctuating, allowing gravity to win the battle for your shoulders and chin.
sensationData.physicalSensation: Heavy eyelids, a slight drooping of the mouth, and a feeling like your clothes are heavier than usual.
sensationData.metaphor: A sail that has lost just enough wind to start fluttering and drooping, but the boat is still moving slowly.
sensationData.theImpulse: To lean your head on your hand, to slump in your chair, and to speak in a quieter, flatter tone.
sensationData.superpower: Micro-Rest. You are catching small pockets of recovery in the middle of the day, preventing a deeper collapse later.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel the 'glum' weight internally. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Ceiling Scan. Lift your chin just enough to see where the wall meets the ceiling in the corner of the room. Notice the angle.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using an upward vertical gaze to reset the vestibular system and provide a mechanical 'lift' cue to the cervical extensors.

mind.text: Saturation Search. Identify one object in the room with a bright or saturated color. Notice how the light defines its shape.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from the 'monochrome' bias of a low-energy state to high-contrast external data, which increases brainstem alertness.

social.text: The Phatic Ping. If someone is nearby, ask a neutral, 'boring' question (e.g., 'Do you know what time it is?'). If alone, text a friend a simple sun emoji ☀️.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using low-stakes social contact to gently nudge the ventral vagal system back into an 'active' state without emotional drain.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world looks a bit gray or heavy. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Posterior Expansion. Inhale and imagine the air filling the back of your lungs, specifically the space between your shoulder blades.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using internal breath volume to provide a 'pneumatic' lift to the upper thoracic spine, counteracting the forward sag.

mind.text: The Gentle Question. Ask your body silently: 'What is one tiny physical adjustment (a pillow, a glass of water) that would feel 1% better right now?'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Stimulating internal agency and a micro-dopamine spark by identifying a high-reward, low-effort improvement to the self.

social.text: The Virtual Support. Visualize a safe person giving you a quick, encouraging nod or a gentle hand on your shoulder. Feel the warmth of that idea.
social.physioTip: Best for: Accessing safe attachment imagery to provide an internal 'pillar' of support, signaling the system that it is safe to re-engage.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Dulled
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel dulled. Disappointment acts like a dampener on the senses, protecting you from the 'sharpness' of a recent letdown.
sensationData.blueprint: Sensory muting. Your nervous system is slightly desensitizing your inputs to help you process a frustration without it feeling overwhelming.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A 'fuzzy' or muffled quality to sounds and colors. A feeling of being slightly behind a thin veil.
sensationData.metaphor: Looking through a window that has a layer of dust on it—the view is there, but the edges are soft and the colors are dim.
sensationData.theImpulse: To stop looking for the 'good' in things and to settle for a neutral, muted experience.
sensationData.superpower: Disappointment Buffer. You are giving your heart time to adjust to a change in plans by not feeling the full 'sting' all at once.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel internal 'static' or dullness. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Long Word Hunt. Scan the room for the longest word you can find on a book, sign, or screen. Read it slowly to yourself.
body.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the prefrontal cortex in a high-focus visual task to break the emotional 'fuzziness' and pull awareness into the room.

mind.text: Color Matching. Name three specific, separate objects in the room that are the exact same color. Focus on the shade.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the visual processing system to increase its contrast-detection, alerting the brain to external detail.

social.text: The Verbal Seal. Whisper one word that describes your current disappointment. Listen to the sound of your own voice in the air.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the vibration of the vocal cords to 'pierce' the internal silence and mark your physical presence in the environment.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels flat or unreachable. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Cutaneous Ping. Firmly pinch the skin on the back of your hand or forearm. Focus entirely on the sharp point of pressure.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing high-contrast, localized tactile input to the somatosensory cortex, forcing the brain back into the 'container' of the skin.

mind.text: Thermal Contrast. Find something cold (metal) and something warm (your own neck). Alternate touching them and notice the distinct difference.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing temperature receptors to provide strong, distinct signals that interrupt the 'flat' feeling of dissociation.

social.text: Passive Curiosity. Look at an object or person nearby and mentally identify one specific texture (e.g., 'that sweater is knit', 'that table is slick').
social.physioTip: Best for: Re-establishing a safe, low-demand relational tether to the physical world through sensory observation.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Sluggish
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel sluggish. Your system is moving slowly to carefully navigate a situation where you feel a bit guarded or uncertain.
sensationData.blueprint: High-viscosity movement. Your system is increasing the 'resistance' in your motor circuits to ensure you don't move faster than your current sense of safety allows.
sensationData.physicalSensation: Movement feels 'thick'. Your thoughts are moving at a slower tempo, and your reaction time feels slightly delayed.
sensationData.metaphor: An engine running with oil that is a bit too thick; it works, but every gear shift takes a bit more effort.
sensationData.theImpulse: To take the easiest path, to procrastinate, and to move in 'slow motion'.
sensationData.superpower: Deliberate Pace. By moving slowly, you are less likely to make a mistake or miss a subtle cue in your environment.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'slow' and heavy internally. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Smooth Pursuit. Find a slowly moving object in the room (a clock hand, a swaying plant, a distant car). Follow it with your eyes for 15 seconds.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using external motor-tracking to 'drag' the brain out of its internal slow-tempo loop and re-engage the brainstem with the outside world.

mind.text: Process Description. Identify one simple physical process happening in the room (e.g., 'the fan is spinning', 'the light is reflecting'). Name it silently.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the 'Thinking Brain' to synchronize with external objective reality, breaking the internal 'viscosity' of thought.

social.text: The Tempo Ping. Text a friend a 'slow' animal emoji. Acknowledging your pace to the world removes the internal pressure to 'speed up'.
social.physioTip: Best for: Externalizing your metabolic state to reduce social anxiety, which often adds a 'bracing' tax to an already sluggish system.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too bright or fast. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Bilateral Tap. Sit firmly. Tap your fingers on your lap, alternating left and right, to a steady, slow rhythm. Focus on the 'touch' on each leg.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using bilateral alternating stimulation to pull a 'spaced out' brain back into its midline and coordinate the hemispheres.

mind.text: The Counted Breath. Inhale (1), Exhale (2), until you reach 10. If you lose count, start over at 1. Keep your focus inside the ribs.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Providing a closed-loop internal track for the brain to follow, anchoring the self back into the biological center.

social.text: The Buffer Request. If in conversation, ask: 'Could you say that again? I want to make sure I'm taking it all in.' Own your processing speed.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using active communication to create a 'social buffer,' allowing your internal speed to catch up with external data.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Foggy
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel foggy. Apathy is a way of protecting yourself from 'caring' too much when you feel vulnerable or over-exposed.
sensationData.blueprint: Cognitive clouding. A mild dissociative state where the brain reduces the 'resolution' of your thoughts to prevent emotional overwhelm.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A 'cloud' behind the forehead. Difficulty finding specific words and a sense of being 'spaced out' while still awake.
sensationData.metaphor: Driving through a light mist; you can see the road, but the signs are hard to read until you are right on top of them.
sensationData.theImpulse: To shrug your shoulders, to say 'I don't know' or 'I don't care', and to avoid complex tasks.
sensationData.superpower: Mental Buffer. The fog acts like a soft cushion, preventing sharp thoughts or criticisms from landing too hard while you are feeling vulnerable.
sensationData.generalGoal: Clearing the windshield. One clear point.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'cloudy' and lost in the internal mist. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Near-Far Accommodation. Hold your thumb close to your face. Focus on it for 3 seconds, then focus on an object at the furthest edge of the room. Repeat 3 times.
body.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the ciliary muscles to adjust, which sends an immediate 'update' signal to the brainstem to sharpen environmental awareness.

mind.text: The Literal Fact. State one objective truth about the room out loud: 'The floor is wood' or 'There is a lamp.' Listen to the sound of your voice.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using the 'Thinking Brain' to label objective reality, which pulls consciousness out of a dissociative drift and into the room.

social.text: The Color Note. Focus on the literal, high-detail color of a safe person’s clothing or an object they own for 5 seconds.
social.physioTip: Best for: Establishing an external micro-tether through high-detail observation, bypassing the 'fog' of apathy.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels blurry and you feel detached. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Clearing Breath. Take a deep inhale and exhale with a loud, sharp 'Ha!' sound. Feel the vibration in your chest and belly.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using forced exhalation to stimulate the diaphragm and the vagus nerve, signaling the brain to re-occupy the core of the body.

mind.text: The One-Task Anchor. Identify the single, most basic physical need you have right now (e.g., 'I need to adjust my seat' or 'I need a sip of water').
mind.physioTip: Best for: Reducing cognitive load by collapsing 'foggy' external data into a single, binary internal action.

social.text: Sharp Edge Trace. Find one object with a very sharp, clear edge. Trace that edge with your eyes while silently saying: 'I am here, and that is there.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Using high-contrast boundaries to define the 'Self vs. Not-Self,' anchoring a spaced-out brain back into its own skin.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Exploding
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel like you are exploding. Your system has detected a critical boundary violation or threat and has flooded your engine with every drop of high-octane fuel to ensure your survival.
sensationData.blueprint: Sympathetic Overload. The amygdala has hijacked the steering wheel, shutting down logic to provide maximum power to your limbs and lungs for an immediate fight-or-flight response.
sensationData.physicalSensation: Extreme heat in the face and chest; a racing heart that feels like it might burst; jaw muscles clenched; and an overwhelming urge to move or strike.
sensationData.metaphor: A solar flare or a steam boiler where the pressure gauge has hit the red line and the safety valve is screaming.
sensationData.theImpulse: To strike out, to scream at the top of your lungs, or to physically destroy the perceived obstacle in your path.
sensationData.superpower: Total Mobilization. You have access to a level of raw, explosive power that can move mountains. Your body is ready to protect you at any cost.
sensationData.generalGoal: Mechanical cooling. Discharge the heat immediately.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are consumed by internal heat and a pounding heart. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Panoramic Vision. Stop fixating on your internal sensations. Look up and soften your gaze to take in the widest possible view of the room's edges.
body.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from 'internal threat-tunneling' to peripheral vision, which sends a hardware signal to the brainstem to inhibit the sympathetic surge.

mind.text: The Blue Search. Find and name 5 blue objects in the room. Focus purely on the literal color, not what the objects are.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the prefrontal cortex to engage with cold, external data, pulling resources away from the internal 'rage' circuits.

social.text: The Boundary Declaration. If safe, say out loud to the room: 'I am running too hot. I am stepping away for 5 minutes.' Then physically leave the space.
social.physioTip: Best for: Exercising social agency to create physical distance, which tells the brain the 'fight' is being managed through space rather than impact.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside (Fixated)
pathB.goal: You are hyper-fixated on the trigger. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Isometric Wall Push. Place both hands flat against a wall. Push with 100% of your strength for 10 seconds, feeling the tension in your own arms and legs.
body.physioTip: Best for: Discharging motor energy into the skeleton and a neutral object. This simulates 'fight' completion while pulling awareness back into the muscles.

mind.text: The Physiological Sigh. Take two sharp inhales through the nose, followed by one long, forceful exhale through the mouth. Feel your ribs move.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Mechanically popping the alveoli in the lungs to rapidly offload CO2 and pull the 'emergency brake' on the heart rate from the inside.

social.text: The Energy Vent. If with a safe person, ask: 'I need to shake this out. Can you just watch me for a second?' Then shake your hands and feet vigorously.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using 'witnessed discharge' to anchor the explosive energy within the safe container of a relationship, pulling the focus back to your own body's release.

sensationData.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

sensationData.title: Flooded
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel flooded. Your sensory inputs are overflowing, and your brain can no longer process the 'data'—it only sees 'danger'. You are not losing control; you are being overwhelmed by your own survival energy.
sensationData.blueprint: Sensory Saturation. Your nervous system is receiving more input than your 'processor' can handle, leading to a state of high-arousal confusion and panic.
sensationData.physicalSensation: Short, shallow breaths; a feeling of 'pins and needles' or tingling; dizziness; and a sense that the room is spinning or closing in.
sensationData.metaphor: A river that has burst its banks during a storm. The water is everywhere, and the current is too fast to stand in.
sensationData.theImpulse: To run blindly, to flail, to hyperventilate, and to find an immediate exit at any cost.
sensationData.superpower: Hyper-Vigilance. In this state, you are noticing everything. Your system is trying to find a gap in the chaos to lead you toward safety.
sensationData.generalGoal: Lowering the water level. Rhythmic anchoring.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are drowning in internal panic sensations. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Rhythmic Stomp. Stomp your feet firmly on the ground: Left, Right, Left, Right. Focus entirely on the vibration traveling through your leg bones.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using 'bone conduction' and high-intensity feedback to provide an undeniable external anchor when internal borders feel lost.

mind.text: 5-4-3-2-1 Sensory Scan. Name 5 things you can see, 4 you can hear, and 3 you can touch in this exact room right now.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the 'Thinking Brain' to cycle through external sensory channels, which disrupts the internal panic loop and re-engages the prefrontal cortex.

social.text: Co-Regulated Mirroring. If a safe person is nearby, look at their shoulders. Try to match the slow, steady rhythm of their movement.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing mirror neurons to 'outsource' your regulation to a stable external nervous system, pulling you out of your internal chaos.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels loud, chaotic, and 'too much'. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Tunnel Vision Control. Cup your hands around your eyes like goggles to block out the room. Focus only on your own hands or a single spot on the floor.
body.physioTip: Best for: Manually reducing the 'visual tax' on the brain by limiting peripheral data, allowing the system to process one internal point at a time.

mind.text: The Internal Sound Anchor. Focus entirely on the sound of your own breath moving in and out of your nose. Count each exhale silently.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using a rhythmic, predictable internal signal to synchronize a racing, chaotic internal tempo.

social.text: Bi-Polar Containment. Place one hand firmly on your forehead and the other on your chest. Squeeze slightly and feel the boundaries of your own body.
social.physioTip: Best for: Providing a physical sense of 'closure' and boundary to the core, helping a 'spaced out' brain re-inhabit the somatic container.

sensationData.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

sensationData.title: Overload
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel overloaded. You have too many tabs open in your brain, and your system is beginning to 'glitch' under the pressure. You aren't failing; you are simply at maximum capacity.
sensationData.blueprint: Cognitive Congestion. High sympathetic arousal is forcing your brain to try and solve 10 problems at once, leading to a decrease in executive function and an increase in agitation.
sensationData.physicalSensation: Tightness across the forehead and temples; shallow breathing in the upper chest; and a 'buzzing' feeling in the brain that makes it hard to focus.
sensationData.metaphor: A switchboard where every single light is blinking at once and the wires are starting to smoke.
sensationData.theImpulse: To snap at anyone who speaks to you, to work faster and faster despite making mistakes, and to feel 'done' with everything.
sensationData.superpower: High-Speed Processing. Your brain is trying to be a super-computer. It is attempting to protect your future by solving every possible problem right now.
sensationData.generalGoal: Slowing the CPU. One thing at a time.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: Your mind is a racing loop of 'must-dos'. Goal: Externalizing the List.
