mind.text: The Achievement Audit. Identify 3 objects in the room that represent 'success' or 'reliability' to you (a project you finished, a sturdy wall, a tool you use well). Notice their exact position in space.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building exteroceptive capacity. It uses the Mind Lever to map 'Success Cues' in the environment, reinforcing the brain's data that the world is a launching pad.

social.text: The Upward Gaze. Imagine yourself as the 'upward draft' for others. Visualize your confident energy helping someone else rise to a challenge. If possible, give someone an enthusiastic 'thumbs up'.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever to discharge 'lift'. It trains the brain to use high-confidence arousal for social leadership rather than letting it turn into internal pressure.

pathB.title: Path B: Anchored Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels like a launching pad. Goal: Train IN.

body.text: The Power Zip-Up. Stand tall and place your hands on your hips. Imagine 'zipping up' your core from your pubic bone to your throat. Feel the vertical strength of your spine and the solidness of your heels.
body.physioTip: Best for: Strengthening the connection to the midline (The Body Lever). It forces the 'world-striding' brain to acknowledge the visceral reality of its own structural power.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Hot Air Balloon. Say silently: 'As the Balloon, I have released the weight. I am rising with purpose, and my basket (my center) is solid and safe.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It turns an 'upbeat' mood into a structural identity of 'Stable Rise,' preventing the peak from becoming ungrounded.

social.text: The Internal Cheer. Close your eyes for 3 seconds. Offer a sincere compliment to your own effort: 'I am doing a great job.' Feel exactly where that internal praise 'lands' in your chest.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses self-directed prosody to pull external 'Upbeat' vibes back into the physical center, cementing the Confidence state.

sensationData.title: Widening
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel a widening. You’ve just encountered something wonderful or novel, and your system is 'opening the gates' to take it all in.
sensationData.blueprint: The 'Awe' Response. A high-energy state where the brain rapidly expands its world-model to accommodate a new, positive experience, resulting in wide eyes and an open posture.
sensationData.physicalSensation: Wide, bright eyes; a slight gasp of air; and a feeling of 'bigness' in the chest and forehead.
sensationData.metaphor: A telescope lens opening up to its widest setting to capture the light of a thousand stars at once.
sensationData.theImpulse: To say 'Wow!', to stare with wonder, and to move closer to the source of the surprise.
sensationData.superpower: Awe. When you are in a widening state, your ego shrinks and your connection to the world grows. You are experiencing the magic of the 'Infinite'.
sensationData.generalGoal: Holding the wonder. Mastering the Flow.

pathA.title: Path A: Anchored Inside

body.text: Panoramic Vision. Soften your gaze and try to see the entire room at once, including the far corners and the ceiling, without moving your eyes. Take in the 'whole' volume.

mind.text: The Novelty Audit. Identify one thing in the room that you usually ignore. Look at it as if you’ve never seen it before—notice its texture, its shadow, and its color as if for the first time.

social.text: The Universal Link. Visualize the 'bigness' of the universe. Feel how safe it is to be a small, joyful part of a vast, working whole. Mentally share this 'wow' with the world.

social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses self-connection to pull external sensory beauty back into a physical anchor, cementing the Blissful state.

sensationData.title: Bouncy
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel bouncy. You have a surplus of light energy that wants to be expressed through movement and play. Your system is 'spring-loaded' for joy.
sensationData.blueprint: Light Sympathetic Engagement. A playful state where the 'Vagal Brake' is released just enough to allow for rhythmic, bouncy movements without any threat response.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A lightness in the heels; a desire to tap your toes; and a 'springy' feeling in your muscles, as if you are ready to jump for joy.
sensationData.metaphor: A tennis ball dropped from a small height—hitting the ground and popping back up with effortless, repetitive energy.
sensationData.theImpulse: To fidget playfully, to use 'animated' gestures, to joke, and to move with a little extra 'pep'.
sensationData.superpower: Playfulness. This is the foundation of creativity. Your 'bounciness' allows you to look at problems as games and people as playmates.
sensationData.generalGoal: Expressing the pep. Mastering the Flow.

pathA.title: Path A: Anchored Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a fun internal 'spring'. Goal: Train OUT.

body.text: Space-Time Bridging. While gently bouncing your knees or heels, open your eyes and focus on your fingertips. Look at a colorful object 15 feet away. Finally, look at the furthest horizon or wall possible.
body.physioTip: Best for: Exercising neural flexibility. It prevents 'Bounciness' from turning into internal 'Fidgety Anxiety' by tethering your internal rhythm to the actual physical volume of the room.

mind.text: The Playground Scan. Identify 3 objects in the room that look 'fun,' 'colorful,' or 'light.' Notice their exact location and how they might be used in a game.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building exteroceptive capacity. It uses the Mind Lever to map 'Play Cues' in the environment, reinforcing the brain's data that the world is as light as the body feels.

social.text: The Joy-Signal. Visualize your 'bouncy' energy traveling outward like a friendly, rhythmic pulse. Text a friend a playful emoji (🐰, 🦘) or share a quick joke. Imagine their nervous system catching your 'pep'.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever to expand the perimeter. It trains the brain to use high-safety energy for social co-regulation and creative bonding.

pathB.title: Path B: Anchored Outside

body.text: Fascial Recoil Bounces. Stand up and do 5 light, easy bounces on the balls of your feet. Focus entirely on the 'elastic' recoil of your calves and the loose, heavy feeling of your arms.
body.physioTip: Best for: Strengthening the connection to the midline (The Body Lever). It forces the 'world-playing' brain to acknowledge the visceral mechanics of its own springy anatomy.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Tennis Ball. Say silently: 'As the Tennis Ball, I am resilient and light. I turn every impact into an upward movement. My energy is effortless, and my center is round and safe.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It turns a 'playful' mood into a structural identity of 'Dynamic Resilience'.

social.text: The Joy-Jump Anchor. Close your eyes for 3 seconds. Imagine yourself doing something fun. Identify the exact spot in your feet or legs where the 'bounce' starts. Breathe into that spot.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses self-connection to pull external 'fun' vibes back into a physical anchor, cementing the Playful state.

sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel a 'rising' sensation. You are moving out of a lower state and into a higher one. Your 'internal elevator' is going up.
sensationData.blueprint: Ventral Shift. Your system is moving from 'protection' toward 'connection' or from 'rest' toward 'readiness'. It is the biological feeling of improvement.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A subtle lifting in the chest; an opening in the throat; and the feeling that your eyes are looking slightly more 'upward' than before.
sensationData.metaphor: A hot air balloon just as the burner is turned on—the fabric starts to fill, the ropes go taut, and the basket begins to lift off the grass.
sensationData.theImpulse: To take a deep, satisfying breath, to stand a little taller, and to plan something for the near future.
sensationData.superpower: Optimism. When you are rising, you are naturally tuned to hope. You can see the 'upward' path even in difficult circumstances.
sensationData.generalGoal: Nurturing the lift. Mastering the Flow.

pathA.title: Path A: Anchored Inside
pathA.goal: You feel an internal 'upward' momentum. Goal: Train OUT.

body.text: Space-Time Bridging. While feeling your internal lift, open your eyes and focus on the tip of your nose. Look at an object 15 feet away. Finally, look at the highest point where the wall meets the ceiling, or out toward the furthest horizon.
body.physioTip: Best for: Exercising neural flexibility. It prevents 'Rising' from becoming a disconnected daydream by tethering your internal lift to the actual vertical volume of the room.

mind.physioTip: Best for: Building exteroceptive capacity. It uses the Mind Lever to map 'Growth Cues' in the environment, reinforcing the brain's data that the world is moving upward with you.

social.text: The Hopeful Signal. Say silently: 'Things are moving in a good direction.' Visualize this hopeful energy extending like a soft upward draft to someone else. If possible, tell someone: 'I'm feeling hopeful about [X].'
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever to expand the perimeter. It trains the brain to use high-safety hope as a signal of shared progress and social cohesion.

pathB.title: Path B: Anchored Outside

body.text: The Sternum Lift. Take a deep breath and imagine it lifting your chest and sternum directly toward the ceiling. Feel the expansion in the front of your ribs. Maintain 10% of that lift as you exhale.
body.physioTip: Best for: Strengthening the connection to the midline (The Body Lever). It forces the 'promise-focused' brain to acknowledge the visceral mechanics of its own postural opening.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Balloon. Say silently: 'As the Balloon, I am filling with light air. My center is rising, and I see the path ahead clearly. I am light, and I am safe.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It turns a 'hopeful' mood into a structural identity of 'Stable Elevation'.

social.text: The Internal Anticipation. Close your eyes for 3 seconds. Name one small thing you are looking forward to. Identify exactly where that 'anticipation' lives in your body and breathe into it.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses self-connection to pull external 'promise' back into a physical anchor, cementing the Hopeful state.

sensationData.title: Brimming
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel brimming. You are full to the edge with quiet joy. Your 'cup' isn't just full; it’s about to overflow in the best possible way.
sensationData.blueprint: Saturated Ventral State. Your body budget is completely balanced and there is a surplus of 'safety' neurochemicals (oxytocin and serotonin) creating a feeling of emotional wealth.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A 'full' feeling in the heart area; a constant, easy half-smile; and a sense that you are 'radiating' warmth from your core.
sensationData.metaphor: A fountain just as the water reaches the very top edge of the basin—perfectly still for a second, then beginning to trickle over the side.
sensationData.theImpulse: To share a kind word, to be generous with your time, and to linger in the presence of others.
sensationData.superpower: Abundance. When you are brimming, you have so much 'good' inside that it naturally spills over into your actions. You are a source of effortless kindness.
sensationData.generalGoal: Holding the fullness. Mastering the Flow.

pathA.title: Path A: Anchored Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a rich, quiet joy internally. Goal: Train OUT.

body.text: Space-Time Bridging. While feeling your internal 'fullness,' open your eyes and focus on your palms. Look at an object 15 feet away. Finally, look at the furthest horizon or wall possible.
body.physioTip: Best for: Exercising neural flexibility. It prevents 'Brimming' from turning into internal 'Nostalgia' by tethering your emotional wealth to the actual physical volume of the room.

mind.text: The Perfection Scan. Look around the room and find one detail that looks 'perfect' or 'complete' just as it is. Notice its exact position and how it fits into the space.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building exteroceptive capacity. It uses the Mind Lever to map 'Symmetry and Completion' cues in the environment, reinforcing the brain's data that the world is as 'full' as the body.

social.text: The Joy-Pour. Visualize yourself 'pouring' a little bit of this internal joy into a future task or meeting. Imagine your presence making the space feel warmer. If possible, offer a sincere 'thank you' to someone nearby.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever to expand the perimeter. It trains the brain to use high-safety energy for social stewardship rather than keeping it as a private loop.

pathB.title: Path B: Anchored Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like a place that 'belongs' to you. Goal: Train IN.

body.text: The Presence Press. Place your hands together on your lap or desk. Press them together with 10% effort. Focus entirely on the sensation of your own warmth and the 'fullness' of your physical presence.
body.physioTip: Best for: Strengthening the connection to the midline (The Body Lever). It forces the 'environmentally-satisfied' brain to acknowledge the visceral mechanics of the joy.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Basin. Say silently: 'As the Basin, I am solid and wide. I hold the wealth of this moment, and my spillover is a gift to the world. I am full and I am safe.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It turns a 'joyful' mood into a structural identity of 'Resilient Abundance'.

social.text: The Spark Anchor. Close your eyes for 3 seconds. Identify the one 'spark' or thought that started this feeling. Feel exactly where that 'click' lives in your chest and breathe into it.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses self-connection to pull external belonging back into a physical anchor, cementing the Joyful state.

sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel smooth. Your system is moving with zero friction. You are alert, safe, and perfectly synchronized with your environment.
sensationData.blueprint: Flowing Homeostasis. The sympathetic and parasympathetic branches are perfectly balanced, allowing for 'effortless action' and clear, calm cognition.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A lack of 'tension-knots'; easy, fluid movements; and a sense that your thoughts are gliding rather than jumping.
sensationData.metaphor: A luxury car cruising on a freshly paved road—no bumps, no noise, just a steady, high-quality forward motion.
sensationData.theImpulse: To work steadily, to talk with a melodic tone, and to handle the day's tasks with 'easy' competence.
sensationData.superpower: Grace. When you are smooth, you handle life's transitions without jarring. You are efficient, elegant, and effective.
sensationData.generalGoal: Maintaining the glide. Mastering the Flow.

pathA.title: Path A: Anchored Inside
pathA.goal: You feel a lovely internal 'evenness'. Goal: Train OUT.

body.text: Space-Time Bridging. While holding your internal 'glide', open your eyes and focus on the skin of your wrists. Look at a level surface 15 feet away (like a table edge). Finally, look at the furthest horizon or wall possible.
body.physioTip: Best for: Exercising neural flexibility. It prevents 'Smooth' from becoming a static 'internal freeze' by tethering your ease to the actual physical volume of the world.

mind.text: The Balance Audit. Look around the room and identify 3 objects that look 'balanced' or 'level' (a shelf, a picture frame, a flat surface). Notice how their structural stillness mirrors your internal state.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building exteroceptive capacity. It uses the Mind Lever to map 'Stability Cues' in the environment, reinforcing the brain's data that the world is as friction-free as the body.

social.text: The Melodic Signal. Enjoy the 'smooth' feeling of just being yourself. Imagine your voice and movements traveling out like soft ripples in water. Greet someone with a melodic, low-friction tone.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever to expand the perimeter. It trains the brain to use high-safety energy for social synchronization and rhythmic bonding.

pathB.title: Path B: Anchored Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels predictable and harmonious. Goal: Train IN.

body.text: The Infinity Glide. Move your dominant hand in a slow 'infinity' (figure-eight) pattern in the air. Focus entirely on the fluid movement of your shoulder and elbow. Ensure there is zero 'jerk' in the movement.
body.physioTip: Best for: Strengthening the connection to the midline (The Body Lever). It forces the 'environmentally-synced' brain to acknowledge the visceral mechanics of its own fluid joint-gliding.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Luxury Cruise. Say silently: 'As the Cruise, I am moving forward with ease and quality. My engine is quiet, my road is clear, and I handle every turn with grace.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It turns a 'pleasant' mood into a structural identity of 'Stable Efficiency'.

social.text: The Tactile Glide. Run your palm over a smooth surface (table, fabric, or your own arm). Focus entirely on the lack of resistance. Identify where that 'frictionless' feeling lives in your thoughts.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses self-connection to pull external harmony back into a physical anchor, cementing the Pleasant state.

sensationData.title: Paralyzed
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel paralyzed. Your system has detected a threat so immense that it has chosen total immobility as your safest survival strategy.
sensationData.blueprint: Total biological lockdown. The sympathetic drive to run is perfectly balanced by the dorsal vagal drive to disappear, resulting in a 'statue' state.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A heavy, leaden feeling in the limbs combined with a high-frequency internal buzzing. Muscles are locked but high-voltage.
sensationData.metaphor: A computer that has frozen mid-task with the fan spinning at maximum speed.
sensationData.theImpulse: To stop breathing, to fix your gaze on one point, and to disappear internally.
sensationData.superpower: The Ultimate Stillness. You are conserving every ounce of life-force by becoming a non-target.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are trapped in the internal gridlock. Goal: Tiny Motor Spark.

body.text: The Pinky Pulse. Focus entirely on your right pinky finger. Try to wiggle it just one millimetre. Repeat 5 times.
body.physioTip: Best for: Initiating 'micro-mobilization' by recruiting the smallest motor units, proving to the brain that the 'lockdown' is not total.

mind.text: The Time Anchor. Silently name the current day of the week, the month, and the year. Repeat them three times.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Re-engaging the prefrontal cortex with objective temporal data to break the 'timeless' feeling of the freeze response.

social.text: Shadow Edge Trace. Find one shadow on the wall. Slowly trace its edges with your eyes. Notice where the light meets the dark.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using neutral visual tracking to pull the gaze out of a 'fixed stare' without requiring the stress of social interaction.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like a dangerous, frozen photograph. Goal: Sensory Thaw.

body.text: The Conscious Blink. Slowly close your eyes, feel the skin of your eyelids touch, and slowly open them. Repeat 3 times.
body.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'frozen gaze' of a high-arousal freeze by re-introducing the most basic rhythmic motor action of the face.

mind.text: The Weight Shift. Notice the exact point where your body is touching the chair or the floor. Is it heavy or light? Just observe for 5 seconds.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Re-establishing a connection to the 'midline' and gravity, which signals the dorsal system that it is supported.

social.text: Wall Support. If you can, lean one shoulder or your back against a solid wall. Feel the pressure of the wall 'holding' your frame.
social.physioTip: Best for: Outsourcing the need for 'bracing' to the environment, providing the tactile data of a solid boundary while in a vulnerable state.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Purple Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Buried
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel buried. The weight of your environment feels like it is physically pressing down on you, and your system is huddling underneath it to protect your core.
sensationData.blueprint: Compressive Freeze. A high-intensity state where the nervous system 'folds' under pressure. You are conserving energy by occupying the smallest possible space.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A crushing weight on the chest and shoulders; shallow, constricted breathing; and a feeling of being 'sunk' into the floor.
sensationData.metaphor: Being underneath a heavy, weighted blanket that you didn't ask for. It is quiet, but there is no room to move.
sensationData.theImpulse: To curl into a ball, close your eyes, and wait for the weight to be lifted by someone else.
sensationData.superpower: Structural Integrity. Even under immense pressure, your system is holding its shape. You are surviving the squeeze.
sensationData.generalGoal: Space-making. Finding one cubic inch of room.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel crushed by internal heaviness. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Object Naming. Locate 3 hard, unmoving objects in the room (a desk, a door, a heavy lamp). Say their names out loud in a low whisper.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using language to label 'unmoving' external reality, which proves to the brain that the environment is stable even if the self feels crushed.

mind.text: Hard Surface Trace. Find a straight, hard edge in the room (like a shelf or window frame). Trace the line with your eyes from start to finish.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'compressive' mental loop by tethering the visual system to the structural geometry of the room.

social.text: The Shield Signal. Text a 'shield' emoji 🛡️ to a safe person. It signals that you are holding a heavy weight and currently need a protected perimeter.
social.physioTip: Best for: Externalizing the 'oppressed' feeling into a social tether, which reduces the internal 'masking' pressure.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels like it's closing in. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Micro-Rib Expansion. Place your hands on the sides of your ribcage. Take a shallow breath and try to push your ribs against your palms by just 1 millimeter.
body.physioTip: Best for: Initiating 'space-making' in the thoracic container. Small movements signal the brain that the 'squeeze' can be resisted from within.

mind.text: The Sky Anchor. Close your eyes for 3 seconds. Imagine the ceiling or the sky above you lifting by 10 feet. Breathe into that new, imagined height.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using ideokinesis to counteract the 'sinking' sensation, providing the neurological 'illusion' of space to a cramped nervous system.

social.text: The Fist Flash. Clench your fists as tight as you can for 3 seconds, then suddenly spread your fingers wide. Feel the blood rushing back into your skin.
social.physioTip: Best for: Reclaiming the body's 'edges' and motor agency. The rush of blood (vasodilation) provides a visceral 'I am here' signal.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Purple Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Static
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel like static. Your system is receiving too much conflicting 'signal' to process, so it has turned everything into a protective white noise.
sensationData.blueprint: Neural Interference. Your sympathetic (Red) and dorsal (Blue) systems are firing so rapidly that they are cancelling each other out, leaving you in a buzzing haze.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A 'fuzzy' or vibrating feeling in the brain and skin; pixelated vision; and a sense that your focus is constantly jumping.
sensationData.metaphor: An old television tuned to a dead channel. A screen full of buzzing black-and-white dots—lots of energy, but no clear picture.
sensationData.theImpulse: To stare into space, rub your head, and wait for the 'picture' of reality to clear.
sensationData.superpower: Information Filtering. Your system is muting a chaotic world so that you don't have to process the painful details of this moment.
sensationData.generalGoal: Tuning the dial. Finding one clear frequency.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel fuzzy and fragmented internally. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Tactile Dial. Pick up a small object with a distinct texture (a stone, a coin, or a pen). Roll it slowly between your palms, focusing entirely on the sensation against your skin.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using high-contrast tactile feedback to 'tune' the brain's processing away from internal static and toward a specific external coordinate.

mind.text: The Detail Hunt. Find the smallest piece of text in your view (on a book spine, a label, or a screen). Try to read the letters one by one silently.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the foveal visual system to engage in high-detail discrimination, which manually 'sharpens' the neural picture and re-engages the prefrontal cortex.

social.text: Distance Listening. Close your eyes for 5 seconds. Search for the furthest possible sound you can hear *outside* the room (a car, a bird, the wind).
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the 'orienting reflex' to expand the auditory field, pulling the focus out of the 'head-buzz' and into the wider environment.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world looks like a blurry, buzzing mess. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Humming Reset. Take a shallow breath and make a long, low 'Mmmmmm' sound. Focus entirely on the vibration in your lips, teeth, and chest.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using internal resonance to stimulate the vagus nerve and provide an undeniable internal 'frequency' for a fragmented brain to latch onto.

mind.text: The Heartbeat Metronome. Place two fingers on your pulse (wrist or neck). Don't try to change it; just count the 'thump-thump' for 10 seconds.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Providing a concrete biological rhythm to anchor a 'pixelated' consciousness back into the reality of the physical body.

social.text: The Lyric Anchor. Recall the first line of a song you know perfectly. Hum that one line silently to yourself while feeling the air leave your nose.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing familiar auditory memory to create a sense of internal 'order' and sequence, helping the 'static' brain find a coherent narrative.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Purple Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Locked
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel locked. You are in a 'biological' stalemate where your body has decided that any movement at all is too dangerous to attempt.
sensationData.blueprint: Rigid Immobilization. The nervous system has increased muscle spindle sensitivity to the maximum. You are 'braced' for an impact that hasn't ended yet.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A locked jaw; stiff neck; and joints that feel like they have been fused. You may feel like you are 'trapped' inside a suit of stone.
sensationData.metaphor: A high-security vault that has gone into lockdown mode. The doors are bolted, and the code has been temporarily wiped from the system.
sensationData.theImpulse: To hold your breath, to grit your teeth, and to wait for a 'key' or a sign of safety to arrive.
sensationData.superpower: Impenetrability. In this state, nothing can get in to hurt you. You have created an absolute boundary that the outside world cannot cross.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You are a prisoner of your own tension. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Open Search. Identify 3 'open' or 'unlocked' things in the room (e.g., an open door, a gap between books on a shelf, an empty cup, or a window).
body.physioTip: Best for: Priming the brain with visual data of 'openness' and 'passageways' to counteract the internal neurological lockdown.

mind.text: The Safety Gap. If with someone, ask them to sit exactly 3 feet away from you. Focus entirely on the empty space (the gap) between you and them.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'proxemics' to prove to the nervous system that you have a clear perimeter and an exit, which reduces the need for rigid bracing.

social.text: The Perimeter Scan. Slowly move your eyes to find the nearest exit (a door or window). Trace the frame of that exit once with your eyes.
social.physioTip: Best for: Engaging the 'orienting response' toward an exit, which signals the brain that movement is possible, even if it isn't happening yet.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like a cage. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Tongue Unlock. Stick your tongue out as far as possible toward your chin, hold for 1 second, then relax it back. Repeat 3 times.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing the hypoglossal nerve to 'unlock' the floor of the mouth and the throat, which are the primary 'anchors' of the rigid freeze state.

mind.text: Internal Lubrication. Visualize a warm, golden oil being poured over your shoulders and jaw. Imagine it slowly seeping into the 'hinges' of your joints.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using 'ideokinesis' to facilitate a top-down release of muscle spindles by providing the brain with a 'sliding' rather than 'locking' mental model.

social.text: Dental Boundary. Gently press the tip of your tongue against the back of your front teeth. Feel the hard, certain boundary of your own mouth.
social.physioTip: Best for: Establishing a tactile 'Self-boundary' at the midline, helping a spaced-out system re-occupy the 'vault' of the body safely.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Purple Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Compressed
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel compressed. Your system is trying to pack all of your energy into a tiny, dense core to keep it from leaking out or being detected.
sensationData.blueprint: High-Density Freeze. A state of intense internal pressure where the 'volume' of your life-force has been turned down to a single, concentrated point.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A feeling of 'tightness' in the skin; a sense that your internal organs are being squeezed together; and very short, clipped breaths.
sensationData.metaphor: A spring that has been pushed down as far as it can go. It takes up very little space, but it is vibrating with potential energy.
sensationData.theImpulse: To stay small, to talk in a whisper, and to keep your elbows tucked tightly into your sides.
sensationData.superpower: Concentrated Power. You are currently at your most 'dense'. When this compression eventually releases, it will provide a massive burst of mobilizing energy.
sensationData.generalGoal: Releasing the spring—one coil at a time.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'packed in' and stifled. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Length Trace. Place your hands on your hips and slowly slide them down your thighs all the way to your knees. Feel the literal length of your legs.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using long-range tactile feedback to prove to the brain that the 'container' is larger than the 'compressed' internal feeling suggests.

mind.text: The Room Count. Look at the furthest wall. Mentally count how many normal steps it would take for you to walk from where you are to that wall.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from 'internal density' to 'external spatial navigation', which requires the brain to process distance and room volume.

social.text: The Window Expansion. Look at a window or door. Acknowledge the vast, uncompressed space that exists outside these specific walls. Just notice it.
social.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'claustrophobic' freeze by tethering the visual system to a much larger environmental horizon.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels too small/cramped. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Nasal Hiss. Take a tiny inhale through your nose, then exhale through your nose with a soft, slow 'hiss' sound. Feel the internal pressure drop 1%.
body.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing nasal resistance to slowly offload pressure from the thoracic cavity without the 'exposure' of an open-mouth breath.

mind.text: The Ripple Expansion. Imagine the 'point' of energy in your chest getting just a tiny bit wider, like a single ripple moving outward in a calm pond.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using ideokinesis to facilitate a 'micro-thaw' of the fascial retraction, signaling the core that it is safe to expand by millimeters.

social.text: The Vocal Anchor. Say one single word out loud. Any word (e.g., 'Stone', 'Blue', 'Now'). Just feel the air and vibration leave your throat.
social.physioTip: Best for: Re-engaging the laryngeal nerves to break the 'stifled' silence of the freeze, reclaiming the midline voice.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Purple Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Constricted
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel constricted. Your system is tightening its grip to prevent any 'leakage' of energy that might be detected. You are a biological fortress holding its breath.
sensationData.blueprint: Vaso-constrictive Freeze. A low-mobilization trap where the body narrows its focus and physical presence to the absolute minimum to survive a perceived threat.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A tight band around the chest; a 'closed' throat; and a feeling of being squeezed from all sides by an invisible force.
sensationData.metaphor: An airlock that has been sealed shut. There is just enough air to survive, but no room for anything else to enter or leave.
sensationData.theImpulse: To swallow repeatedly, to pull your shoulders to your ears, and to minimize your breathing.
sensationData.superpower: Ductile Resilience. Even when squeezed to your limit, your core remains intact. You are proving your ability to endure under pressure.
sensationData.generalGoal: Micro-expansion. Finding one thin straw of air.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel an internal 'clamping' down. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Corner Expansion. Find the furthest corner of the room from where you are sitting. Look at it steadily and acknowledge the vast volume of air that exists between you and that corner.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using visual-spatial data to counteract the 'airlock' sensation, proving to the brain that the environment is expansive even if the chest feels tight.

mind.text: Space Counting. Look around the room and count exactly 5 'openings' or 'gaps' (e.g., a doorway, the space between chair legs, an open window, a gap between books).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from 'internal constriction' to 'external permeability,' which requires the brain to process pathways and exits.

social.text: The Cloud Signal. Text a 'cloud' emoji ☁️ to a safe person. It signals that you are feeling 'breathless' or tight and need a quiet, spacious social perimeter.
social.physioTip: Best for: Externalizing the feeling of constriction into a low-stakes social tether, reducing the metabolic cost of trying to 'act normal'.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The air in the room feels 'thin' or tight. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Lip Flutter. Gently press your lips together and exhale just enough to make them vibrate (like a horse 'brrr') for 2 seconds. Focus on the tickle in your lips.
body.physioTip: Best for: Sending a vibration through the laryngeal and pharyngeal branches of the vagus nerve, which can mechanically 'un-stick' the constriction in the throat.

mind.text: Balloon Ribs. Visualize your lungs as two soft, colorful balloons. As you take a tiny inhale, imagine them expanding just one millimeter wider into the space of your chest.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using ideokinesis to gently re-introduce movement to the intercostal muscles without triggering the 'startle-brace' of a deep breath.

social.text: Internal Permission. Close your eyes for 3 seconds. Say silently to your own lungs: 'It is okay to take up space. You are allowed to breathe right now.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'self-directed' prosody to lower the defensive tone of the respiratory muscles, pulling awareness back into the safety of the breath.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Purple Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Rigid
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel rigid. Your muscles have turned into 'armor' to protect your vitals. You are biologically mimicking the safety of a stone.
sensationData.blueprint: Tonic Immobility. A high-tension trap where the muscles are fully 'loaded' but the 'Go' signal is blocked by a massive 'Stop' command.
sensationData.physicalSensation: Limbs that feel like wood or cold metal; a stiff neck that won't turn; and a 'plank-like' feeling in the core.
sensationData.metaphor: A knight in a suit of armor that has rusted shut. The strength is there, but the movement is gone.
sensationData.theImpulse: To stay perfectly still, to hide your hands, and to keep your eyes fixed on the 'threat'.
sensationData.superpower: Stony Defense. You have become a hard target. In this state, your system is maximizing its physical durability to survive an impact.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: You feel 'bolted' internally. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Tactile Softness. Find the softest texture within reach (clothing, hair, or a cushion). Touch it with only your fingertips. Focus entirely on the 'give' of the material.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing the brain with 'low-resistance' tactile data to counteract the internal feeling of being 'hard as stone', pulling awareness into the room's softer edges.

mind.text: Motion Observation. Find one thing in the room that is currently moving (a clock hand, a leaf outside, the flicker of a light). Watch it for 10 seconds.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Re-syncing the visual system with external rhythm and 'flow,' which proves to the brain that the world is moving even if the self is frozen.

social.text: The Safety Gap. If with someone, ask them to sit nearby but *not* touch you. Focus on the empty, safe space (the buffer) between you and them.
social.physioTip: Best for: Establishing a clear perimeter. Knowing there is a 'gap' reduces the need for defensive armoring and allows the social engagement system to peek out.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like a physical wall. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Wrist Ripple. Focus on your wrists. Try to move them in the smallest possible circle—just a millimeter. If they feel fused, simply *imagine* the circle moving inside the joint.
body.physioTip: Best for: Re-introducing the concept of 'oscillation' to a rigid system. Even imagined movement can start to inhibit the tonic muscle-spindle firing from within.

mind.text: The Pliable Mantra. Repeat the word 'Pliable' slowly in your mind. Imagine the word itself is soft and flexible, bending around the edges of your thoughts.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using semantic priming to shift the brain's 'motor-model' from 'Rigid/Hard' to 'Flexible/Soft', encouraging a top-down release of the armoring.

social.text: Un-Armoring Visualization. Close your eyes for 5 seconds. Imagine yourself standing in a warm, safe room, slowly un-clasping a heavy suit of armor, piece by piece.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using mental imagery to facilitate a systemic release of the 'bracing' reflex by providing a narrative of safety and shedding weight.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Purple Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Seized
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel seized. Your system has 'seized the gears' to prevent a catastrophic breakdown. It is a protective emergency halt.
sensationData.blueprint: Neural Gridlock. The high-energy sympathetic drive and the high-energy dorsal shutdown have collided, stalling the engine completely.
sensationData.physicalSensation: A sharp, sudden 'stop' in the body; wide, fixed eyes; and a feeling of internal 'screeching' that results in no movement.
sensationData.metaphor: A car engine that has run out of oil and fused its parts together. The power was there, but the friction became too much.
sensationData.theImpulse: To gasp and hold it, to stare with 'dinner-plate' eyes, and to stay in a state of shock.
sensationData.superpower: The Emergency Brake. You have successfully stopped a dangerous escalation by pulling the plug on all movement. You are safe in the stall.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: Your internal engine is 'stalled' and hot. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Visual Refresh. Blink your eyes as rapidly as you can for 5 seconds. This forces the visual cortex to 're-map' the room and stop the fixed-stare shock.
body.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'dinner-plate' eye stare of high-arousal shock by manually cycling the eyelid reflex and refreshing retinal data.

mind.text: Neutral Fixation. Find one object that is completely neutral—neither good nor bad (e.g., a plain floor tile, a chair leg, a blank wall). Look at it for 10 seconds.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Giving the 'Aghast' brain a low-arousal target to process, preventing the visual system from continuing to scan for the source of the shock.

social.text: The Cold Sink. Reach out and touch a solid, cold surface (like a metal table leg or a stone wall). Focus entirely on the temperature transfer.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using thermal contrast to ground the 'vibrating' energy of shock into an unmoving external anchor.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The world feels like a startling 'flash'. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Manual Heartbeat. Gently tap your own chest or thigh in a slow, steady 1-2, 1-2 rhythm. Focus on the physical 'ping' of your finger hitting your body.
body.physioTip: Best for: Re-introducing an intentional rhythm to a 'stalled' system, signaling the brain that it is safe to return to a sequential, rhythmic state.

mind.text: Temporal Anchoring. State one fact about the present moment out loud: 'I am in [Location]. The year is [Year].' Hear the sound of your own voice.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Using the prefrontal cortex to 'timestamp' reality, which breaks the frozen 'out-of-time' feeling of a high-intensity freeze response.

social.text: Vocal Visualization. Close your eyes for 3 seconds. Imagine a safe person saying your name in a very calm, steady, and low voice. Feel the sound in your chest.
social.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing auditory memory to prime the social engagement system, pulling the 'seized' brain back into a state of internal co-regulation.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Purple Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Gridlocked
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you feel gridlocked. You have an enormous amount of energy ready to fight or flee, but the 'gates' are locked tight. You are a volcano with the lid on.
sensationData.blueprint: Active Sympathetic-Dorsal Conflict. You are at the maximum point of 'Mobilized Immobility'. You are ready to explode, but you are choosing to implode instead for safety.
sensationData.physicalSensation: Extreme internal pressure; a pounding in the ears; vibrating muscles; and a feeling that you might 'burst' if you move an inch.
sensationData.metaphor: A city intersection where four lanes of traffic have met in the middle and no one can move. The horns are honking, but everything is stuck.
sensationData.theImpulse: To scream into a pillow, to clench every muscle until you shake, or to 'bolt' blindly.
sensationData.superpower: Infinite Potential. You are holding a massive amount of power right now. When it is safe to release it, you will be capable of incredible action.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The internal pressure is at the 'red line'. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The High-Contrast Press. Press your palms together as hard as you can for 5 seconds. Then, suddenly drop your hands and imagine the 'push' leaving your fingertips into the floor.
body.physioTip: Best for: Discharging high-voltage motor energy into an isometric 'clash,' providing an externalized exit for the gridlocked sympathetic drive.

mind.text: The Grain Search. Find 5 objects in the room made of wood or stone. Focus entirely on the detail of the grain or the texture of the surface.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Forcing the brain to process complex, high-detail external data, which pulls focus out of the internal 'vibrating' pressure loop.

social.text: The Paper Crumple. Write or draw the 'Horror' or the pressure on a scrap of paper. Crumple it into the smallest ball possible and throw it away from you.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using a physical metaphor to externalize 'trapped' emotion, providing a safe motor completion for the impulse to 'get it out'.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: The environment feels like a trigger for explosion. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Low Hum Vent. Take a shallow breath. Exhale through your mouth with a low, vibrating 'Hummmm' sound until you are empty. Feel the vibration in your teeth.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using the 'vibratory valve' of the vocal cords to bleed off high-intensity sympathetic energy while re-occupying the midline.

mind.text: The Gauge Visualization. Close your eyes for 3 seconds. Imagine an internal pressure gauge. Visualize the needle slowly moving out of the deep Red and back into the Orange.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Utilizing 'top-down' cognitive control to re-frame the interoceptive feedback of pressure as a manageable, shifting variable.

social.text: The Vocal Release. Say one word out loud that represents 'solid' (e.g., 'Stone', 'Wall', 'Here'). Focus on the feeling of the air leaving your throat.
social.physioTip: Best for: Breaking the 'Struck' silence by reclaiming the voice. It proves to the internal system that the 'lid' can be opened safely.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Purple Zone can feel scary.

sensationData.title: Shaking
sensationData.validation: It makes sense that you are shaking. This is your body's attempt to discharge a massive amount of trapped survival energy. It is the beginning of the 'thaw'.
sensationData.blueprint: The Freeze-Thaw transition. Your system is finally moving the high-voltage Red energy that was locked under the Blue brake.
sensationData.physicalSensation: Involuntary tremors, chattering teeth, or a feeling of internal vibration or 'shivering' without being cold.
sensationData.metaphor: A pressure valve finally whistling as the steam begins to escape. The water is still hot, but the pressure is dropping.
sensationData.theImpulse: To hide the shaking, to freeze harder, or to feel 'out of control'.
sensationData.superpower: The Great Discharge. Shaking is the 'Master Key' your nervous system uses to unlock itself and return to life.
sensationData.generalGoal: Allowing the flow. Finding the container.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: The shaking feels overwhelming or scary from the inside. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Structural Anchoring. Stand or sit against a solid wall. Press your hands or back firmly into it. Allow the shaking to happen while focusing entirely on the unmoving support of the building.
body.physioTip: Best for: Providing an external 'immovable' reference point to prove to the brain that while the body is moving, the world is stable.

mind.text: Counting the Beats. Observe the rhythm of the tremors for 10 seconds. Try to count them like a musician (1-2-3-4). Is the frequency fast, slow, or uneven?
mind.physioTip: Best for: Shifting from 'being shaken' to 'observing movement,' which moves the brain from the brainstem back to the analytical prefrontal cortex.

social.text: Silent Witness. If with a safe person, ask them to just sit nearby and watch. If alone, visualize a kind, steady person watching the process without trying to fix it.
social.physioTip: Best for: Reducing the 'shame-alarm' that often stops a discharge. Knowing there is a 'steady witness' helps the system complete the thaw safely.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: You feel detached (dissociated). Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Blanket Container. Wrap yourself tightly in a blanket or give yourself a very firm 'self-hug' (hands to opposite shoulders). Squeeze until you feel your own ribs.
body.physioTip: Best for: Using deep pressure (DTP) to define the body's 'envelope,' helping the brain re-tether to the physical self during a high-arousal discharge.

mind.text: Vocal Matching (VOO). Make a low, long 'Vooooo' sound. Try to match the vibration of your voice to the frequency of the shaking in your chest or jaw.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Harmonizing the involuntary discharge with an intentional vocal vibration, signaling the brain that the movement is 'organized' and safe.

social.text: Mirroring. If with a safe person, ask them to gently shake their own hands along with you. If alone, shake your hands intentionally for 3 seconds, then let the involuntary tremor take over.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using social mirroring or intentional motor-mimicry to pull the 'spaced-out' brain back into the rhythm of the body.

sensationData.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Purple Zone can feel scary.

intensityTheory.zoneName: Tired but Wired (The Freeze / The Trap)
intensityTheory.sensations: Shaking, Gridlocked, Seized, Rigid, Constricted, Compressed, Locked, Static, Buried, Paralyzed
intensityTheory.emotions: Terrified, Invisible, Dissociated, Trapped, Stifled, Breathless, Petrified, Fragmented, Struck, Distressed
intensityTheory.validation: It makes sense that you feel terrified. Your system is doing exactly what it was designed to do to survive a worst-case scenario.
intensityTheory.blueprint: You have stepped into the biological trap. Your system perceives an inescapable threat so that it has pressed the gas pedal (Red) and the emergency brake (Blue) at the exact same time.
intensityTheory.physicalSensation: Externally, you look completely still, or spaced out, but internally, your heart is pounding and your muscles are braced. It is the sensation of being 'tired but wired'.
intensityTheory.metaphor: An engine revving at 7,000 RPM while the wheels are locked in concrete, or a high-pressure pipe with all the valves welded shut.
intensityTheory.theImpulse: To completely freeze, stop breathing, or disconnect from your body.
intensityTheory.superpower: The ultimate pain-killer. Your nervous system immobilized you so you would not draw the attention of a predator, while simultaneously flooding you with endorphins so you won't feel the pain of an inescapable attack.
intensityTheory.generalGoal: Sensory Ignition. Discharge the Lock. Do not use logic. Mechanically burn off the chemical lock to release the brake.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside (Rigid)
pathA.goal: Locked in high muscular tension. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Neurogenic Shake. Stand up or stay seated. Vigorously shake your hands, arms, and legs for 60 seconds. Imagine you are shaking water off your skin. Let your jaw hang loose. Focus on the 'exit' of energy through your fingertips.
body.physioTip: Best for: Discharging the sympathetic load. As a physio, you know this 'shaking' mimics the natural mammalian release of trapped adrenaline, signaling the brain that the 'Fight' is over.

mind.text: The Exhale 'Vooooo'. Take a deep breath. On the exhale, make a low, vibrating 'Vooooo' sound. Focus 100% on the vibration in your lips and chest. Empty your lungs completely. Do this 3 times.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Vagal Stimulation. The 'Vooooo' sound creates a mechanical vibration that stimulates the laryngeal branch of the Vagus nerve, manually 'poking' the brake to release.

social.text: The Silent Witness. Look at a photo of a trusted person or pet. Acknowledge their existence without needing to speak. Visualize their calm energy as a safe 'container' for your shaking.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever as an anchor. It provides a 'Safety Signal' via the visual system to counter the internal 'Frightened' vital.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside (Deer in Headlights)
pathB.goal: Paralyzed by an external threat. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Cut the Visual. Cover your eyes with your palms or close them tightly. Take the world away. Focus entirely on the feeling of your hands against your face and the darkness.
body.physioTip: Best for: Reducing Sensory Overload. In the Freeze state, the visual system is often 'locked' on a threat; cutting the feed allows the brain to pivot to internal safety data.

mind.text: The Archetype: The Thawing Ice. Say silently: 'I am the ice. I am hard and still, but the sun is coming. I am allowed to melt. One drop at a time, I am returning.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It validates the 'Rigidity' while providing a gentle, non-threatening narrative for the 'Thaw'.

social.text: The Sound Anchor. With eyes closed, find one safe, rhythmic sound (a clock, a fan, your own breath). Focus 100% on its tempo. Identify where that rhythm lands in your body.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses an external rhythmic anchor to pull the 'paralyzed' brain back into a predictable, internal pulse.

intensityTheory.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Purple Zone can feel scary. As the 'Freeze' melts, you will likely feel the 'Fight/Flight' (Red) agitation that was trapped underneath.

intensityTheory.sensations: Hibernating, Immobilized, Hollow, Empty, Deflated, Frozen, Shutdown, Numb, Drained, Lethargic
intensityTheory.emotions: Despairing, Hopeless, Desolate, Spent, Drained, Despondent, Depressed, Sullen, Exhausted, Fatigued
intensityTheory.validation: It makes sense that you feel shut down or depleted. Your body is in energy-conserving stasis.
intensityTheory.blueprint: Everything feels voided out, senses numb, motivation evaporated.
intensityTheory.physicalSensation: A profound, immovable heaviness, a drop in body temperature, or the literal feeling of an empty chest cavity.
intensityTheory.metaphor: A dried-up riverbed where everything has gone dormant to survive the cold.
intensityTheory.theImpulse: To fade away, become completely invisible, and detach.
intensityTheory.superpower: Total metabolic conservation. Your system masterfully strips away all excess to preserve core vitality.
intensityTheory.generalGoal: Sensory Ignition. Micro-sparks only. No heavy cognitive lifting when the tank is empty.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside (The Void)
pathA.goal: Consumed by internal heaviness or depletion. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Friction Spark. Press your palms together firmly and rub them fast. Focus 100% on the heat generating between them. Look at your hands as you do it. Feel the 'zing' of the friction.
body.physioTip: Best for: Igniting the periphery. As a physio, you know that thermal and tactile friction provide a 'high-priority' signal to the brain, forcing it to look back 'out' at the skin boundary.

mind.text: The Color Tether. Look around the room. Find and name 3 things that have the same color. Move your eyes to each one and notice their exact shape.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Occipital Engagement. It uses the Mind Lever to 'tether' the brain to external geometry, preventing it from sinking deeper into the internal 'Void'.

social.text: The Silent Visual. Look at a photo of a trusted person or pet. Acknowledge their existence. Visualize their steady, safe presence acting as a 'buoy' on the surface while you are deep underwater.
social.physioTip: Best for: Non-verbal Co-regulation. It provides a visual safety signal that bypasses the need for the 'offline' social-speech circuits.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside (The Fog)
pathB.goal: Spaced out, foggy, or staring blankly. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Sensory Shock. Take three sharp, fast 'sniffing' inhales through the nose, then exhale slowly. Alternatively, splash cold water on your wrists. Focus entirely on the sharp 'sting' or 'cold' inside your nostrils/skin.
body.physioTip: Best for: Systemic Re-boot. Sharp inhales engage the diaphragm and stimulate the sympathetic nervous system just enough to provide a 'micro-spark' of energy to the brain.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Dormant Seed. Say silently: 'As the Seed, I am resting under the frost. I am small, I am safe, and my life is protected. I will grow when the sun returns.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It validates the 'Shut-down' as a temporary, protective phase rather than a permanent failure.

social.text: The Shoulder Container (Self-Hug). Place your right hand firmly on your left shoulder, and your left hand on your right shoulder. Press in. Mentally trace the shape of your arms. Feel the 'edges' of your own body.
social.physioTip: Best for: Proprioceptive Mapping. It uses the Body Lever to define the 'physical container' of the system, helping the 'spaced-out' brain find its way back into the midline.

intensityTheory.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary. As the 'Freeze' melts, you will likely feel the 'Fight/Flight' (Red) agitation.

intensityTheory.sensations: Disconnected, Collapsed, Withdrawn, Sinking, Exhausted, Fading, Slumped, Shrinking, Heavy, Flat
intensityTheory.emotions: Alienated, Miserable, Lonely, Disheartened, Tired, Pessimistic, Morose, Discouraged, Sad, Bored
intensityTheory.validation: It makes sense that you feel heavy and withdrawn. You are not lazy; your system is signaling that the cost of engaging is too high right now.
intensityTheory.blueprint: Your system is powering down. The thinking brain is flickering, and complex logic is becoming difficult.
intensityTheory.physicalSensation: A deep, aching heaviness in the limbs and chest. Slumped posture and mental fogginess.
intensityTheory.metaphor: A thick fog rolling in that obscures the landscape.
intensityTheory.theImpulse: To cancel plans, pull the covers over your head, and avoid eye contact.
intensityTheory.superpower: Strategic retreat. Your system is intentionally dimming the lights to protect you.
intensityTheory.generalGoal: Gentle Mobilization. Micro-sparks of movement. No heavy cognitive lifting.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside (The Slump)
pathA.goal: Consumed by internal heaviness and the urge to slump. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Postural Reset. Sit or stand as tall as possible. Physically lift your chin and roll your shoulders back. Look at the highest point in the room. Hold this 'Upright' shape for 3 breaths.
body.physioTip: Best for: Inhibiting the Collapse. As a physio, you know that spinal extension inhibits the Dorsal Vagal 'slump' and provides a mechanical signal of 'Ready' to the brainstem.

mind.text: The 5-Object Anchor. Look around and name 5 specific things you can see. Move your eyes slowly to each one. Notice one 'bright' detail about each object (a reflection, a color, a sharp edge).
mind.physioTip: Best for: Occipital Ignition. It uses the Mind Lever to 'tether' the flickering brain to external reality, preventing it from sinking further into the internal fog.

social.text: The Low-Stakes Signal. Send a single emoji (like a 🌊 or ⚓) to a trusted friend. Visualize the message traveling through the air as a tiny spark of connection. No words required.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever to expand the perimeter. It maintains a 'micro-bridge' to the world without demanding the energy cost of a full conversation.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside (The Muffle)
pathB.goal: The world feels distant, muffled, or overwhelming. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Tactile Friction. Rub your palms firmly up and down your thighs for 30 seconds. Focus entirely on the heat and the 'swish' sound of your hands against the fabric. Feel the boundaries of your legs.
body.physioTip: Best for: Proprioceptive Re-entry. It uses the Body Lever to define the 'edges' of the self, helping the 'spaced-out' brain find its way back into the physical container.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Fog-Light. Say silently: 'As the Fog-Light, I am steady and I am here. The fog is thick, but my center is bright. I do not need to see the whole path; I only need to see the next step.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It validates the 'Fogginess' while providing a structural identity of 'Stable Presence' within it.

social.text: Internal Pulse Anchor. Tap your finger against your leg to the exact rhythm of your own heartbeat (or a steady 1-2-1-2 pace). Focus entirely on the impact. Identify where that 'beat' lives in your core.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses rhythmic motor output to pull the 'distant' brain back into a predictable, internal pulse of life.

intensityTheory.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

intensityTheory.validation: It makes sense that your system is slowing down. Your body is simply pausing to protect its resources.
intensityTheory.blueprint: A mild energy drop, gently turning down your metabolic rate. Your thinking brain is still fully online.
intensityTheory.physicalSensation: A slight loss of physical sharpness, a drooping posture, and muffled senses.
intensityTheory.metaphor: A dimmer switch gently turning down the lights in a room.
intensityTheory.theImpulse: To sigh, step back, and passively withdraw.
intensityTheory.superpower: The pause. Allows you to stop expending energy on something that isn't working.
intensityTheory.generalGoal: Cognitive Steering and Light Re-engagement. Mastering the Flow.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside (The Sluggish)
pathA.goal: Feeling the internal sag and apathy. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Posture Signal. Stand up and stretch both arms overhead as high as possible. Reach for the ceiling. Take one deep, deliberate inhale and hold for 2 seconds before a sharp exhale. Look at the horizon.
body.physioTip: Best for: Inhibiting the 'Slump'. As a physio, you know that full shoulder flexion and thoracic extension send an immediate 'Anti-Gravity' signal to the brainstem, breaking the Dorsal 'pull'.

mind.text: The Speed Bump Label. Identify your current feeling and say out loud: 'I am hitting a speed bump, not a wall.' Find 3 objects in the room and name their furthest edge.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Cognitive Steering. Using the 'Voice Lever' to label the state prevents the brain from catastrophizing the low energy into a permanent 'Shutdown'.

social.text: The Low-Effort Bridge. Send a quick, unrelated message to a friend (e.g., a photo of something nearby). Visualize the connection 'waking up' a small part of your social brain.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever to expand the perimeter. It provides a 'Micro-Activation' of the Ventral Vagal system without requiring high-energy output.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside (The Dull)
pathB.goal: The environment feels dull or uninspiring. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Thermal Targeting. Hold a warm cup of tea or run warm water over your wrists. Focus 100% on the exact point where the heat meets your skin. Identify the shape of the warmth.
body.physioTip: Best for: Sensory Re-entry. In the 'Dulled' state, the brain is filtering out input; thermal data is a 'high-priority' signal that forces the brain back into the body's midline.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Dimmer Switch. Say silently: 'As the Dimmer Switch, I am in control of the light. I am low right now to save my filament, but I am still connected to the power. I will turn up when I am ready.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It validates the 'Lowered Intensity' as a functional choice rather than a state of being 'broken'.

social.text: Curiosity Mining. Pick one mundane object in the room and find 3 interesting details you never noticed before. Identify where the 'click' of curiosity lands in your core.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses the 'Mind Lever' to audit the environment for data, pulling the 'unplugged' brain back into a state of active, quiet observation.

intensityTheory.warning: THE THAW WARNING: Leaving the Blue Zone can feel scary.

intensityTheory.sensations: Exploding, Flooded, Overloaded, Twitchy, Surging, Burning, Boiling, Clenched, Braced, Racing
intensityTheory.emotions: Enraged, Panicked, Stressed, Jittery, Shocked, Livid, Furious, Frustrated, Tense, Stunned
intensityTheory.validation: It makes sense that you feel explosive. Your body is flawlessly executing an ancient survival protocol. You are biologically primed for maximum protection.
intensityTheory.blueprint: Sympathetic Hyper-Arousal. Adrenaline flood. The Amygdala has inhibited the Prefrontal Cortex to prioritize immediate physical mobilization (Fight or Flight).
intensityTheory.physicalSensation: Pounding heart; rapid, shallow chest-breathing; intense heat in the face and neck; clenched jaw and fists; 'tunnel vision'.
intensityTheory.metaphor: A raging forest fire or a car engine redlining—the heat is massive and the energy is looking for an exit.
intensityTheory.theImpulse: To explode, to attack, to scream, to smash something, or to immediately flee the area.
intensityTheory.superpower: Ultimate Mobilization. In this state, you have access to peak physical strength and speed. Your system has masterfully prepared you to overcome an inescapable obstacle.
intensityTheory.generalGoal: Mechanical Braking & Energy Discharge. Use the Body Lever to cool the engine.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside (The Boiling)
pathA.goal: Consumed by internal heat and racing heart. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: The Physiological Sigh. Take two sharp inhales through the nose (the second one pops the alveoli open), followed by one very long, slow exhale through the mouth. Repeat 3 times.
body.physioTip: Best for: Offloading CO2 and lowering heart rate. As a physio, you know this specific breathing pattern is the fastest mechanical way to signal the brainstem to re-engage the Vagal Brake.

mind.text: Visual Broadening. Look up and take in the far top corners of the room. Transition to panoramic vision (seeing everything at once without focusing on one thing). Name 5 neutral objects out loud.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Breaking Tunnel Vision. Panoramic vision is neurologically linked to the parasympathetic system; it 'hacks' the brain into a safer state by dilating the visual field.

social.text: The Safe Exit. State clearly: 'My system is running hot. I am stepping away for 5 minutes to regulate. I will be back.' Move your body to a different room immediately.
social.physioTip: Best for: Preventing Relational Damage. Using the 'Voice Lever' to label the physiology (not the emotion) keeps the prefrontal cortex tethered to reality while allowing for a physical discharge.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside (The Fixation)
pathB.goal: Hyper-fixated on the trigger/threat. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: Heavy Isometric Resistance. Find a solid wall. Place your hands on it and push as hard as you can for 10 seconds. Focus 100% on the tension in your triceps and the solidness of the floor. Shake out your arms after.
body.physioTip: Best for: Discharging Sympathetic Energy. This provides the 'Fight' energy with a safe, non-destructive target, allowing the muscles to finish the stress-cycle through isometric contraction.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Controlled Burn. Say silently: 'As the Controlled Burn, I am heat and I am power, but I am contained. I am burning through the old wood to make space for the new. I am the master of the flame.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It validates the 'Burning' sensation as a productive, temporary force rather than a destructive failure of character.

social.text: The Vent Valve. Reach out to a safe person. Ask: 'I am in a Red Zone. Can you listen for 2 minutes while I discharge this? No advice needed.' Identify where the 'relief' of being heard lands in your chest.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses the Social Lever to create a 'Safe Container' for the overflow, helping the brain find its way back to a +1 Safety baseline.

intensityTheory.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

intensityTheory.sensations: Volatile, Trembling, Pressurized, Vibrating, Fidgety, Pounding, Guarded, Scanning, Flushed, Prickly
intensityTheory.emotions: Fuming, Frightened, Angry, Nervous, Restless, Anxious, Apprehensive, Worried, Irritated, Annoyed
intensityTheory.validation: It makes sense that you feel explosive. Your body is flawlessly executing an ancient survival protocol. You are biologically primed for maximum protection.
intensityTheory.blueprint: Sympathetic Hyper-Arousal. Adrenaline flood. The Amygdala has inhibited the Prefrontal Cortex to prioritize immediate physical mobilization (Fight or Flight).
intensityTheory.physicalSensation: Pounding heart; rapid, shallow chest-breathing; intense heat in the face and neck; clenched jaw and fists; 'tunnel vision'.
intensityTheory.metaphor: A raging forest fire or a car engine redlining—the heat is massive and the energy is looking for an exit.
intensityTheory.theImpulse: To explode, to attack, to scream, to smash something, or to immediately flee the area.
intensityTheory.superpower: Ultimate Mobilization. In this state, you have access to peak physical strength and speed. Your system has masterfully prepared you to overcome an inescapable obstacle.
intensityTheory.generalGoal: Mechanical Braking & Energy Discharge. Use the Body Lever to cool the engine.

pathB.goal: Hyper-fixated on the trigger/threat. Goal: Pull IN.

body.physioTip: Best for: Discharging Sympathetic Energy. This provides the 'Fight' energy with a safe, non-destructive target, allowing the muscles to finish the stress-cycle through isometric contraction.

mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It validates the 'Burning' sensation as a productive, temporary force rather than a destructive failure of character.
