intensityTheory.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

intensityTheory.validation: It makes sense that you feel this subtle charge. Your body is giving you a small injection of fuel to deal with what it perceives as a minor challenge or a boundary violation.
intensityTheory.blueprint: Mild Sympathetic Escalation. The 'Early Warning System' is active. The Prefrontal Cortex is fully online and attempting to categorize the source of the unease.
intensityTheory.physicalSensation: Subtle tightening of the shoulders or jaw; a slight quickening of the breath; an urge to move the hands or 'fix' something small.
intensityTheory.metaphor: A yellow traffic light—a signal to stay alert, check your surroundings, and prepare to either speed up or slow down.
intensityTheory.theImpulse: To fidget, to speak more sharply than intended, to overthink a minor detail, or to passively withdraw.
intensityTheory.superpower: Boundary Detection. In this state, you are highly sensitive to misalignments. You have the biological fuel to address a small issue before it becomes a large one.
intensityTheory.generalGoal: Cognitive Steering and Mild Discharge. Mastering the Flow.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside (The Tension)
pathA.goal: Feeling subtle internal tension or 'charge'. Goal: Pull OUT.

body.text: Forward Ambulation. Take a 2-minute walk, even if it's just across the room and back. Focus entirely on the changing visual data as you move. Look at the furthest wall or window.
body.physioTip: Best for: Optokinetic Reset. As a physio, you know that forward movement and lateral eye tracking (looking around while walking) send a massive 'Safety/Exploration' signal to the brain, quieting the threat detector.

mind.text: The Fuel Reframe. Label the feeling out loud: 'My body is giving me the energy I need to handle this.' Find 3 neutral objects in the room and identify one way they are 'useful' or 'stable'.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Cognitive Steering. Using the 'Voice Lever' to re-categorize adrenaline as 'fuel' rather than 'anxiety' keeps the prefrontal cortex in the driver's seat.

social.text: The Reality Check. Ask a safe person: 'I'm feeling a bit charged about [X]—am I reading this situation right?' Listen 100% to their tone of voice. Visualize your energy balancing with theirs.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever to expand the perimeter. It uses external 'Ventral Vagal' feedback to stabilize your own flickering safety baseline.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside (The Annoyance)
pathB.goal: Hyper-focusing on a minor external annoyance. Goal: Pull IN.

body.text: The Vagal Exhale. Take a slow breath in for 3 counts, then extend the exhale through pursed lips for 6 counts. Focus entirely on the sensation of your ribcage gently 'dropping' and softening.
body.physioTip: Best for: Strengthening the connection to the midline (The Body Lever). Forcing the exhale-dominance manually engages the parasympathetic 'brake,' pulling the brain back into the body.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Traffic Light. Say silently: 'As the Yellow Light, I am a moment of transition. I am alert and I am capable. I have the time to choose my speed.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It validates the 'Alertness' as a tool for agency rather than a reaction to a threat.

social.text: The Collaborative Pivot. State clearly: 'I'm finding this a bit frustrating—can we brainstorm a different way to look at this?' Identify where the 'relief' of speaking up lands in your jaw or chest.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses the Social Lever to address the 'annoyance' directly, pulling the 'outward' focus back into a state of personal agency and core calm.

intensityTheory.warning: What do you want to achieve? Do you want to use the energy? Then steer to yellow. Do you want to relax? Calm down into green.

intensityTheory.sensations: Slowing, Grounded, Quiet, Warm, Still, Loose, Steady, Stable, Settled, Light
intensityTheory.emotions: Sleepy, Complacent, Tranquil, Cozy, Serene, Mellow, Thoughtful, Peaceful, Comfortable, Carefree
intensityTheory.validation: Your nervous system is performing critical repairs and refilling your body budget.
intensityTheory.blueprint: Vagal Brake is fully engaged. Body is in physiological safety.
intensityTheory.physicalSensation: Pleasant heaviness, unclenched muscles, effortless breathing.
intensityTheory.metaphor: A high-performance car parked in the garage for a tune-up.
intensityTheory.theImpulse: To sleep, cuddle, reflect, or simply exist.
intensityTheory.superpower: Deep Restoration. Biologically building structural capacity to handle future stress.

pathA.title: Path A: Anchored Inside
pathA.goal: Savoring internal stillness. Goal: Train OUT.

body.text: Space-Time Bridging (Micro). While resting, open your eyes briefly. Focus on the furthest corner of the room or the sky outside for 5 seconds. Acknowledge the 'container' of the room before closing your eyes again.
body.physioTip: Best for: Maintaining spatial awareness. It prevents 'Deep Rest' from turning into 'Dissociative Fog' by keeping a thin, safe tether to the external world.

mind.text: The Boundary Scan. Identify 3 points in the room that represent 'protection' (a wall, a closed door, a heavy curtain). Recognize that the world is holding a safe space for your rest.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building exteroceptive safety. It uses the Mind Lever to confirm the environment is secure, allowing the brain to drop even deeper into repair mode.

social.text: The Lighthouse Presence. Visualize your calm, heavy energy radiating outward like a soft light. Imagine your presence providing a silent 'anchor' of safety for others in the house without needing to speak.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever for co-regulation. It frames 'Rest' as a contribution to the group's collective safety baseline.

pathB.title: Path B: Anchored Outside
pathB.goal: Feeling tranquil and safe in environment. Goal: Train IN.

body.text: Conscious Heaviness. Focus entirely on the parts of your body touching the chair, bed, or floor. Feel the literal weight of your bones sinking into the support. Try to make yourself 5% 'heavier'.
body.physioTip: Best for: Strengthening the connection to the midline (The Body Lever). It forces the 'environmentally-connected' brain to acknowledge the visceral reality of parasympathetic 'sink'.

mind.text: The Baseline Anchor. Explicitly memorize this specific sensation of safety. Say silently: 'This is what repair feels like.' Identify exactly where the 'warmth' or 'stillness' is most intense in your core.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It turns a 'sleepy' mood into a structural 'Safety Template' the brain can recall during high-stress Red Zone moments.

social.text: Regulating Touch. If safe, cuddle a pet or wrap yourself in a weighted blanket. Focus on the tactile pressure and the warmth. Notice how your breath slows down to match the contact.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses external contact to pull the 'Tranquil' vibe back into a physical anchor, cementing the Restorative state.

intensityTheory.sensations: Resting, Calm, Restoring, Present, Balanced, Smooth, Anchored, Satisfied, Expanding, Softening
intensityTheory.emotions: Relaxed, Chill, Restful, Blessed, Balanced, Calm, Secure, Satisfied, Grateful, Touched
intensityTheory.validation: Your environment is safe; your system is down-shifting to prepare for connection.
intensityTheory.blueprint: Transitioning to safety. Heart rate is slowing; social engagement is coming online.
intensityTheory.physicalSensation: Muscles unclenching, posture relaxing, open engagement.
intensityTheory.metaphor: A ship dropping anchor in a calm harbor.
intensityTheory.theImpulse: To sigh in relief, smile gently, or appreciate the environment.
intensityTheory.superpower: Co-Regulation. You have the power to help others feel calm just by being in the room.

pathA.title: Path A: Anchored Inside
pathA.goal: Enjoying internal calm, want to gently connect. Goal: Train OUT.

body.text: The Half-Smile (Ventral Trigger). Intentionally create a tiny, gentle 'Mona Lisa' smile. Focus on the lift at the corners of your mouth. Open your eyes and look at an object 15 feet away.
body.physioTip: Best for: Activating the Social Engagement System. The small facial lift sends a direct safety signal to the brainstem, preparing you for external interaction.

mind.text: The 'Not Wrong' Audit. Look around the room. Mentally name 3 things in the environment that feel 'not wrong' or 'safe' right now. Notice their exact color and texture.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building exteroceptive capacity. It uses the Mind Lever to confirm external safety, reinforcing the 'Chill' internal state with environmental data.

social.text: The Casual Bid. Make a low-stakes, neutral observation to someone nearby (or via text). Visualize your words as a soft, safe 'bridge' extending toward them.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever to expand the perimeter. It trains the brain to maintain its internal 'Blessed' state while engaging in gentle social synchronization.

pathB.title: Path B: Anchored Outside
pathB.goal: Feeling open-hearted and engaged. Goal: Train IN.

body.text: The Extended Exhale. Breathe in for 4, then exhale slowly for 8. Focus entirely on the sensation of the air leaving your lungs and the 'drop' of your shoulders with the breath.
body.physioTip: Best for: Strengthening the connection to the midline (The Body Lever). As a physio, you know the long exhale stimulates the Vagus Nerve to deepen the state of calm.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Anchor. Say silently: 'As the Anchor, I am heavy and steady in the sand. The waves move above me, but my center is unshakeable and deep.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It turns a 'relaxed' mood into a structural identity of 'Stable Connection,' preventing the energy from drifting.

social.text: Prosodic Listening. Focus entirely on the melodic tone of a human voice (or music). Identify where the 'warmth' of that sound lands in your own chest. Feel the resonance.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses external 'Glimmers' to pull the social connection back into a physical anchor, cementing the Secure state.

intensityTheory.emotions: At Ease, Easygoing, Content, Loving, Fulfilled
intensityTheory.validation: Your nervous system has assessed you are safe. You are mastering your gears.
intensityTheory.blueprint: Deceleration. Amygdala is quiet; prefrontal cortex is fully online.
intensityTheory.physicalSensation: Momentum slowing without crashing. Grounded feet, rhythmic breathing.
intensityTheory.metaphor: Shifting off a busy highway onto a scenic neighborhood road.
intensityTheory.theImpulse: To look around the room and take a deep clarifying breath.
intensityTheory.superpower: Smooth Transitions. Clarity to purposefully choose your next move.

pathA.title: Path A: Stuck Inside
pathA.goal: Feeling the internal shift of engine slowing. Goal: Deepen the Anchor.
pathA.body: The Gravity Check. Press your feet into the floor and feel the support.
pathA.mind: The Transition Label. Tell yourself: 'I am safely shifting gears.'
pathA.social: The Inner Circle. Spend 30 seconds visualizing the face of a secure person.

pathB.title: Path B: Stuck Outside
pathB.goal: Highly present in environment. Goal: Active Appreciation.
pathB.body: Visual Broadening. Let your eyes wander and rest on whatever is naturally pleasing.
pathB.mind: Micro-Gratitude. Identify one small thing that is working perfectly.
pathB.social: The Just Because Bid. Send a quick message to a friend requiring no response.

intensityTheory.sensations: Widening, Lifted, Bubbling, Electric, Soaring, Buzzing, Bright, Pulsing, Flowing, Buoyant
intensityTheory.emotions: Surprised, Upbeat, Festive, Exhilarated, Ecstatic, Hyper, Cheerful, Motivated, Inspired, Elated
intensityTheory.validation: Your body has successfully married peak energy with profound safety.
intensityTheory.blueprint: The ultimate hybrid state: high-octane energy with the safety circuit firmly engaged.
intensityTheory.physicalSensation: Buzzing electric energy, weightlessness, wide and bright eyes.
intensityTheory.metaphor: A race car on an open track or a surfer on a massive wave.
intensityTheory.theImpulse: To create, celebrate, and lose yourself in a challenging task.
intensityTheory.superpower: The Generative Drive. The biological home of 'Flow.'
intensityTheory.generalGoal: Channeling and Anchoring. Mastering the Flow.

pathA.title: Path A: Anchored Inside
pathA.goal: Bubbling internal energy, need to point at a target. Goal: Train OUT.

body.text: Space-Time Bridging (High Intensity). While feeling the 'buzz', open your eyes wide. Look at your own hands for 1 breath. Look at a target 20 feet away. Finally, look at the furthest horizon or sky possible. Do this while shadowboxing or dancing for 30 seconds.
body.physioTip: Best for: Exercising neural flexibility. It prevents 'High-Energy' from turning into 'Internal Spinning' by tethering your massive arousal to the actual physical volume of the world.

mind.text: The North Star Declaration. Identify the exact project or person you want to gift this energy to. Declare out loud: 'I am using this electric spark to build [X].' Visualize the energy hitting that target.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building exteroceptive capacity. It uses the Mind Lever to create a 'Mission Container' for the adrenaline surge, preventing it from leaking into impulsivity.

social.text: The Spark Bid. Share your excitement! Tell someone: 'I have so much energy, let's go do [X]!' Imagine your high-safety/high-energy signal acting as a 'battery' for the people around you.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever to expand the perimeter. It trains the brain to use peak-arousal for co-regulation and collaborative momentum.

pathB.title: Path B: Anchored Outside
pathB.goal: Completely absorbed in a task or project. Goal: Train IN.

body.text: The Smile-Check Anchor. While working at speed, intentionally make a wide, genuine smile. Feel the 'lift' in your cheeks and eyes. Hold it for 5 seconds while noticing the 'buoyancy' in your chest.
body.physioTip: Best for: Strengthening the connection to the midline (The Body Lever). As a physio, you know the Zygomatic Major activation sends a 'Safety Confirmed' loop to the brainstem during high output.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Surfer. Say silently: 'As the Surfer, I am riding the power of the wave. I am fast, I am balanced, and I am safe. The wave is the energy; I am the direction.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It turns 'Exhilaration' into a structural identity of 'Stable Agency,' preventing the system from overshooting into Red Zone chaos.

social.text: Team Flow Anchor. Briefly pause and explicitly acknowledge a win. If alone, high-five yourself or the air. If with others, offer a 'Team Flow' signal. Identify where the 'click' of success lands in your core.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses external 'wins' to pull the high-speed drive back into a physical anchor, cementing the Motivated state.

intensityTheory.sensations: Awake, Vital, Eager, Expansive, Humming, Engaged, Locked In, Glowing, Elevated, Tingling
intensityTheory.emotions: Energized, Lively, Excited, Optimistic, Enthusiastic, Pleased, Focused, Happy, Proud, Thrilled
intensityTheory.validation: Your body is providing fuel to pursue your goals and interact safely.
intensityTheory.blueprint: Steady stream of dopamine. Prefrontal cortex is fully online for deep work.
intensityTheory.physicalSensation: Physical readiness, upright posture, heart rate elevated but steady.
intensityTheory.metaphor: A sailor catching a perfect steady wind.
intensityTheory.theImpulse: To build, learn, focus deeply, and collaborate.
intensityTheory.superpower: Sustained Generative Drive. Motivation fueled by creation, not survival.
intensityTheory.generalGoal: Sustaining and Directing. Mastering the Flow.

pathA.title: Path A: Anchored Inside
pathA.goal: Internal drive, humming with optimism. Goal: Train OUT.

body.text: Space-Time Bridging (Medium Intensity). While feeling your internal 'hum', open your eyes and focus on your sternum. Look at an object 15 feet away. Finally, look at the furthest horizon or sky possible. Take one deep, energizing breath while maintaining the tall spine.
body.physioTip: Best for: Exercising neural flexibility. It prevents 'Optimism' from turning into 'Internal Daydreaming' by tethering your drive to the actual physical volume of the room.

mind.text: The 'Get To' Audit. Look around the room. List three things you 'get to' do with this energy right now. Identify exactly where those opportunities exist in your environment.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building exteroceptive capacity. It uses the Mind Lever to map 'Possibility Data' in the environment, reinforcing the brain's data that the world is ready for your contribution.

social.text: The Enthusiasm Share. Reach out to a friend to share a win or a 'spark' idea. Visualize your enthusiastic energy traveling outward like a warm, safe current. Acknowledge how it feels to expand your circle.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever to expand the perimeter. It trains the brain to use its 'Humming' energy for pro-social signaling and collaborative resonance.

pathB.title: Path B: Anchored Outside
pathB.goal: Highly absorbed and dialed in. Goal: Train IN.

body.text: The Rhythmic Pulse. While working, gently tap a 1-2-1-2 rhythm with your feet or squeeze a stress ball. Focus entirely on the muscle recruitment and the 'solidness' of the movement. Feel the rhythm in your spine.
body.physioTip: Best for: Strengthening the connection to the midline (The Body Lever). It forces the 'task-absorbed' brain to acknowledge the visceral mechanics of its own sustained drive.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Sailor. Say silently: 'As the Sailor, I am one with the wind. I have the strength to hold the line and the wisdom to set the course. My path is clear and my center is steady.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It turns a 'productive' mood into a structural identity of 'Reliable Agency,' preventing the system from drifting.

social.text: The Micro-Milestone Anchor. Close your eyes for 3 seconds. Identify one tiny goal you just completed. Feel the 'click' of satisfaction in your core. Say silently: 'This is progress.'
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses self-connection to pull external task-success back into a physical anchor, cementing the Motivated state.

intensityTheory.validation: Your body is providing a safe, low-dose spark to explore and learn.
intensityTheory.blueprint: Vagal brake released slightly. Social engagement looking for fun.
intensityTheory.physicalSensation: Light bubbling in the chest, lifting posture, urge to explore.
intensityTheory.metaphor: A puppy waking from a nap and noticing a favorite toy.
intensityTheory.theImpulse: To joke, ask a curious question, or gently engage.
intensityTheory.superpower: Curiosity & Micro-Play. State for tinkering without pressure.
intensityTheory.generalGoal: Encouraging the Spark. Mastering the Flow.

pathA.title: Path A: Anchored Inside
pathA.goal: Hopeful swelling of energy inside. Goal: Train OUT.

body.text: Space-Time Bridging (Low Intensity). While feeling your internal 'spring', open your eyes and focus on your fingertips. Look at a colorful object 10 feet away. Finally, look at the furthest horizon or wall possible while gently bouncing your knees.
body.physioTip: Best for: Exercising neural flexibility. It prevents 'Hopefulness' from turning into internal 'Spacey-ness' by tethering your light spark to the actual physical volume of the world.

mind.text: The Sensory Savor Scan. Look around and identify 3 visually pleasing or vibrant things in the environment. Notice their exact position and the space between you and them.
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building exteroceptive capacity. It uses the Mind Lever to map 'Vitality Cues' in the environment, reinforcing the brain's data that the world is a playground.

social.text: The Playful Bid. Share a curious thought or send a funny meme. Visualize your playful energy traveling outward like a bright, safe signal. Acknowledge how it feels to invite someone else into your 'vibe'.
social.physioTip: Best for: Using the Social Lever to expand the perimeter. It trains the brain to use its 'Brimming' energy for social co-regulation and rhythmic bonding.

pathB.title: Path B: Anchored Outside
pathB.goal: Drawn to something fun or vibrant. Goal: Train IN.

body.text: The Light Bounce. Stand up or sit tall and do a few light, easy bounces on the balls of your feet or toes. Focus entirely on the 'springy' recoil of your tendons and the loose, heavy feeling of your arms.
body.physioTip: Best for: Strengthening the connection to the midline (The Body Lever). As a physio, you know this 'fascial loading' at low intensity provides a massive safety signal to the brainstem.

mind.text: Archetype Integration: The Explorer. Say silently: 'As the Explorer, I am light on my feet and open in my mind. Every detail is an invitation, and my center is safe and curious.'
mind.physioTip: Best for: Building metacognitive capacity. It turns a 'joyful' mood into a structural identity of 'Receptive Agency,' preventing the energy from becoming ungrounded.

social.text: The Curious Anchor. Close your eyes for 3 seconds. Ask yourself: 'What am I most excited about right now?' Identify exactly where that 'click' of curiosity lives in your chest and breathe into it.
social.physioTip: Best for: Interoceptive Integration. It uses self-connection to pull external 'fun' vibes back into a physical anchor, cementing the Playful state.
